2026-01-26T17:10:30.724113Z.question: give me drug sales by week for recent 52 weeks

query_decomposition.intent_summary: Return weekly drug sales totals (sum of qty_sold_pu) for the most recent 52 weeks relative to the latest week_end_date in the dataset.

subqueries.name: max_date_cte
subqueries.purpose: Identify the most recent week_end_date in the dataset
subqueries.logic: Compute MAX(week_end_date) from drug_sales

subqueries.name: rolling_window_start_cte
subqueries.purpose: Determine the start date for the 52-week rolling window
subqueries.logic: Subtract 51 weeks from the max week_end_date to get the inclusive 52-week window start date

validation_rules: Rolling 52-week window must be calculated relative to MAX(week_end_date) in the data
Do not use system date; derive the window from data only
Window should be inclusive: week_end_date between (max_date - INTERVAL 51 WEEK) and max_date
Group by week_end_date only to produce weekly totals
Use SUM(qty_sold_pu) as the sales metric
Do not attempt to fill missing weeks; return only weeks present in the data

2026-01-26T17:10:30.724113Z.final_sql:
WITH max_date_cte AS (
  SELECT MAX(week_end_date) AS max_date
  FROM drug_sales
),
rolling_window_start_cte AS (
  SELECT DATE_SUB(max_date, INTERVAL 51 WEEK) AS start_date
  FROM max_date_cte
)
SELECT s.week_end_date, SUM(s.qty_sold_pu) AS total_sales
FROM drug_sales s
CROSS JOIN max_date_cte m
CROSS JOIN rolling_window_start_cte r
WHERE s.week_end_date BETWEEN r.start_date AND m.max_date
GROUP BY s.week_end_date
ORDER BY s.week_end_date ASC;

2026-01-26T17:38:49.635578Z.question: How much did drug sales grow in the recent 4 weeks

query_decomposition.intent_summary: Compute drug sales growth over the most recent 4 distinct weeks versus the 4 distinct weeks immediately preceding them, based on week_end_date.

subqueries.name: max_date_cte
subqueries.purpose: Identify the most recent week_end_date in the dataset
subqueries.logic: Compute MAX(week_end_date) from drug_sales

subqueries.name: last_4_weeks_cte
subqueries.purpose: Determine the most recent 4 distinct week_end_date values
subqueries.logic: Select the 4 most recent DISTINCT week_end_date values where week_end_date <= (SELECT max_date FROM max_date_cte), ordered DESC, LIMIT 4

subqueries.name: prior_4_weeks_cte
subqueries.purpose: Determine the 4 distinct week_end_date values immediately preceding the last 4 weeks
subqueries.logic: Select DISTINCT week_end_date values where week_end_date < (SELECT MIN(week_end_date) FROM last_4_weeks_cte), ordered DESC, LIMIT 4

subqueries.name: last_4_sales_cte
subqueries.purpose: Aggregate total units sold in the last 4 weeks window
subqueries.logic: Sum qty_sold_pu from drug_sales where week_end_date IN (SELECT week_end_date FROM last_4_weeks_cte)

subqueries.name: prior_4_sales_cte
subqueries.purpose: Aggregate total units sold in the prior 4 weeks window
subqueries.logic: Sum qty_sold_pu from drug_sales where week_end_date IN (SELECT week_end_date FROM prior_4_weeks_cte)

subqueries.name: final_calc
subqueries.purpose: Compute absolute and percentage growth between the two windows
subqueries.logic: Select last_4_sales, prior_4_sales, (last_4_sales - prior_4_sales) AS absolute_growth, CASE WHEN prior_4_sales > 0 THEN (last_4_sales - prior_4_sales) / prior_4_sales ELSE NULL END AS percent_growth

validation_rules: Use MAX(week_end_date) to anchor the most recent period; do not use system date.
Define weeks by distinct week_end_date values, not by a 28-day calendar range.
The prior window must consist of the 4 distinct week_end_date values immediately preceding the minimum date of the last_4_weeks_cte.
If fewer than 4 distinct weeks exist in either window, aggregate over whatever distinct weeks are available.
If prior_4_weeks_sales is 0 or NULL, percent_growth must be NULL to avoid division by zero.
All calculations must reference drug_sales.week_end_date for weekly granularity.

2026-01-26T17:38:49.635578Z.final_sql:
WITH
max_date_cte AS (
  SELECT MAX(week_end_date) AS max_date
  FROM drug_sales
),
last_4_weeks_cte AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT week_end_date
  FROM drug_sales
  WHERE week_end_date <= (SELECT max_date FROM max_date_cte)
  ORDER BY week_end_date DESC
  LIMIT 4
),
prior_4_weeks_cte AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT week_end_date
  FROM drug_sales
  WHERE week_end_date < (SELECT MIN(week_end_date) FROM last_4_weeks_cte)
  ORDER BY week_end_date DESC
  LIMIT 4
),
last_4_sales_cte AS (
  SELECT SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS last_4_weeks_sales
  FROM drug_sales
  WHERE week_end_date IN (SELECT week_end_date FROM last_4_weeks_cte)
),
prior_4_sales_cte AS (
  SELECT SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS prior_4_weeks_sales
  FROM drug_sales
  WHERE week_end_date IN (SELECT week_end_date FROM prior_4_weeks_cte)
)
SELECT
  l.last_4_weeks_sales,
  p.prior_4_weeks_sales,
  (l.last_4_weeks_sales - p.prior_4_weeks_sales) AS absolute_growth,
  CASE
    WHEN p.prior_4_weeks_sales > 0 THEN (l.last_4_weeks_sales - p.prior_4_weeks_sales) / p.prior_4_weeks_sales
    ELSE NULL
  END AS percent_growth
FROM last_4_sales_cte l
CROSS JOIN prior_4_sales_cte p;

2026-01-26T17:53:02.071814Z.question: how much drug grown in recent 4 weeks and how that compared with growth in recent 8 weeks

query_decomposition.intent_summary: Compute drug unit sales growth for the most recent 4 weeks versus the preceding 4 weeks and for the most recent 8 weeks versus the preceding 8 weeks (anchored to the max week_end_date), then compare the two growth rates by reporting their percentage-point difference and indicating whether the 4-week growth rate is higher or the 8-week growth rate is higher.

subqueries.name: max_date_cte
subqueries.purpose: Identify the most recent week_end_date in the dataset to anchor rolling windows.
subqueries.logic: Compute MAX(week_end_date) from drug_sales.

subqueries.name: week_ranks_cte
subqueries.purpose: Create a ranked list of distinct week_end_date values from most recent to older to define contiguous week windows.
subqueries.logic: Select DISTINCT week_end_date where week_end_date <= max_date; assign ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY week_end_date DESC) AS rn so rn=1 is the most recent week.

subqueries.name: w4_current_sum
subqueries.purpose: Total units sold in the most recent 4 distinct weeks.
subqueries.logic: Sum qty_sold_pu from drug_sales where week_end_date IN (select week_end_date from week_ranks_cte where rn BETWEEN 1 AND 4).

subqueries.name: w4_prior_sum
subqueries.purpose: Total units sold in the 4 weeks immediately preceding the most recent 4 weeks.
subqueries.logic: Sum qty_sold_pu from drug_sales where week_end_date IN (select week_end_date from week_ranks_cte where rn BETWEEN 5 AND 8).

subqueries.name: w8_current_sum
subqueries.purpose: Total units sold in the most recent 8 distinct weeks.
subqueries.logic: Sum qty_sold_pu from drug_sales where week_end_date IN (select week_end_date from week_ranks_cte where rn BETWEEN 1 AND 8).

subqueries.name: w8_prior_sum
subqueries.purpose: Total units sold in the 8 weeks immediately preceding the most recent 8 weeks.
subqueries.logic: Sum qty_sold_pu from drug_sales where week_end_date IN (select week_end_date from week_ranks_cte where rn BETWEEN 9 AND 16).

subqueries.name: final_assembly
subqueries.purpose: Assemble growth metrics for 4-week and 8-week windows and compare them via percentage-point difference and direction.
subqueries.logic: Select w4_current_units, w4_prior_units, w8_current_units, w8_prior_units. Compute growth_4w_abs = w4_current_units - w4_prior_units. Compute growth_4w_pct = CASE WHEN w4_prior_units <> 0 THEN (w4_current_units - w4_prior_units) / w4_prior_units ELSE NULL END. Compute growth_8w_abs = w8_current_units - w8_prior_units. Compute growth_8w_pct = CASE WHEN w8_prior_units <> 0 THEN (w8_current_units - w8_prior_units) / w8_prior_units ELSE NULL END. Compute comparison_growth_pct_diff = CASE WHEN growth_4w_pct IS NULL OR growth_8w_pct IS NULL THEN NULL ELSE (growth_4w_pct - growth_8w_pct) END. Compute growth_rate_comparison = CASE WHEN growth_4w_pct IS NULL OR growth_8w_pct IS NULL THEN 'Insufficient data' WHEN growth_4w_pct > growth_8w_pct THEN 'R4W higher' WHEN growth_4w_pct < growth_8w_pct THEN 'R8W higher' ELSE 'Equal' END.

validation_rules: Use MAX(week_end_date) from the data; do not use the system date.
Windows must use distinct week_end_date values and be contiguous and non-overlapping (1-4 vs 5-8; 1-8 vs 9-16).
Guard against division by zero for percentage growth using CASE/NULLIF; if prior is zero, set growth rate to NULL.
For the comparison, compute percentage-point difference as growth_4w_pct minus growth_8w_pct (not a ratio).
Set comparison direction to 'R4W higher', 'R8W higher', 'Equal', or 'Insufficient data' based on the two growth rates.

2026-01-26T17:53:02.071814Z.final_sql:
WITH
max_date_cte AS (
  SELECT MAX(week_end_date) AS max_week
  FROM drug_sales
),
week_ranks_cte AS (
  SELECT d.week_end_date,
         ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY d.week_end_date DESC) AS rn
  FROM (
    SELECT DISTINCT week_end_date
    FROM drug_sales
    WHERE week_end_date <= (SELECT max_week FROM max_date_cte)
  ) AS d
),
w4_current_sum AS (
  SELECT SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS w4_current_units
  FROM drug_sales
  WHERE week_end_date IN (SELECT week_end_date FROM week_ranks_cte WHERE rn BETWEEN 1 AND 4)
),
w4_prior_sum AS (
  SELECT SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS w4_prior_units
  FROM drug_sales
  WHERE week_end_date IN (SELECT week_end_date FROM week_ranks_cte WHERE rn BETWEEN 5 AND 8)
),
w8_current_sum AS (
  SELECT SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS w8_current_units
  FROM drug_sales
  WHERE week_end_date IN (SELECT week_end_date FROM week_ranks_cte WHERE rn BETWEEN 1 AND 8)
),
w8_prior_sum AS (
  SELECT SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS w8_prior_units
  FROM drug_sales
  WHERE week_end_date IN (SELECT week_end_date FROM week_ranks_cte WHERE rn BETWEEN 9 AND 16)
),
final_assembly AS (
  SELECT
    t.w4_current_units,
    t.w4_prior_units,
    t.growth_4w_abs,
    t.growth_4w_pct,
    t.w8_current_units,
    t.w8_prior_units,
    t.growth_8w_abs,
    t.growth_8w_pct,
    CASE
      WHEN t.growth_4w_pct IS NULL OR t.growth_8w_pct IS NULL THEN NULL
      ELSE t.growth_4w_pct - t.growth_8w_pct
    END AS comparison_growth_pct_diff,
    CASE
      WHEN t.growth_4w_pct IS NULL OR t.growth_8w_pct IS NULL THEN 'Insufficient data'
      WHEN t.growth_4w_pct > t.growth_8w_pct THEN 'R4W higher'
      WHEN t.growth_4w_pct < t.growth_8w_pct THEN 'R8W higher'
      ELSE 'Equal'
    END AS growth_rate_comparison
  FROM (
    SELECT
      COALESCE(w4c.w4_current_units, 0) AS w4_current_units,
      COALESCE(w4p.w4_prior_units, 0) AS w4_prior_units,
      (COALESCE(w4c.w4_current_units, 0) - COALESCE(w4p.w4_prior_units, 0)) AS growth_4w_abs,
      CASE
        WHEN COALESCE(w4p.w4_prior_units, 0) = 0 THEN NULL
        ELSE (COALESCE(w4c.w4_current_units, 0) - COALESCE(w4p.w4_prior_units, 0)) / COALESCE(w4p.w4_prior_units, 0)
      END AS growth_4w_pct,
      COALESCE(w8c.w8_current_units, 0) AS w8_current_units,
      COALESCE(w8p.w8_prior_units, 0) AS w8_prior_units,
      (COALESCE(w8c.w8_current_units, 0) - COALESCE(w8p.w8_prior_units, 0)) AS growth_8w_abs,
      CASE
        WHEN COALESCE(w8p.w8_prior_units, 0) = 0 THEN NULL
        ELSE (COALESCE(w8c.w8_current_units, 0) - COALESCE(w8p.w8_prior_units, 0)) / COALESCE(w8p.w8_prior_units, 0)
      END AS growth_8w_pct
    FROM w4_current_sum w4c
    CROSS JOIN w4_prior_sum w4p
    CROSS JOIN w8_current_sum w8c
    CROSS JOIN w8_prior_sum w8p
  ) AS t
)
SELECT
  w4_current_units,
  w4_prior_units,
  growth_4w_abs,
  growth_4w_pct,
  w8_current_units,
  w8_prior_units,
  growth_8w_abs,
  growth_8w_pct,
  comparison_growth_pct_diff,
  growth_rate_comparison
FROM final_assembly;

2026-01-27T05:59:51.334111Z.question: what is the sales growth in recent 4 weeks compared to previous 2 weeks

query_decomposition.intent_summary: Compute weekly-average sales growth by comparing the most recent 4 weeks' average weekly units sold to the immediately preceding 2 weeks' average weekly units sold, anchored to the maximum week_end_date in the dataset.

subqueries.name: max_week_date_cte
subqueries.purpose: Identify the most recent week_end_date in the dataset to anchor rolling windows.
subqueries.logic: Compute MAX(week_end_date) from drug_sales

subqueries.name: week_ranks_cte
subqueries.purpose: Rank distinct weeks relative to the max week_end_date and retain the 6 most recent.
subqueries.logic: Select DISTINCT week_end_date and assign DENSE_RANK ordered by week_end_date DESC starting at 1 for the max; keep ranks 1 through 6

subqueries.name: window_classification_cte
subqueries.purpose: Classify the 6 most recent weeks into two windows: recent 4 weeks (ranks 1-4) and previous 2 weeks (ranks 5-6).
subqueries.logic: Join drug_sales to week_ranks_cte on week_end_date; map ranks 1-4 to 'recent_4w' and ranks 5-6 to 'prev_2w'; filter to ranks <= 6

subqueries.name: weekly_sales_cte
subqueries.purpose: Aggregate total qty_sold_pu per distinct week within each window.
subqueries.logic: From window_classification_cte, group by window_flag and week_end_date; compute weekly_total = SUM(qty_sold_pu)

subqueries.name: window_weekly_avg_cte
subqueries.purpose: Compute average weekly sales and week counts for each window.
subqueries.logic: From weekly_sales_cte, group by window_flag; compute avg_weekly_qty = AVG(weekly_total) and weeks_in_window = COUNT(*)

subqueries.name: avg_growth_cte
subqueries.purpose: Pivot window averages into columns and compute absolute and percentage change based on weekly averages.
subqueries.logic: Transform window_weekly_avg_cte into columns recent_4w_avg_weekly_sales and previous_2w_avg_weekly_sales; compute absolute_change_avg = recent_4w_avg_weekly_sales - previous_2w_avg_weekly_sales; compute percent_change_avg = CASE WHEN previous_2w_avg_weekly_sales = 0 THEN NULL ELSE (recent_4w_avg_weekly_sales - previous_2w_avg_weekly_sales) / previous_2w_avg_weekly_sales END

validation_rules: Anchor all windows to MAX(week_end_date) from the dataset; do not use system date.
Define 'recent 4 weeks' as the four most recent distinct week_end_date values (ranks 1-4) and 'previous 2 weeks' as the next two (ranks 5-6).
Compute weekly averages by first aggregating SUM(qty_sold_pu) per distinct week, then averaging those weekly totals within each window.
If fewer than 4 or 2 weeks exist, use the available distinct weeks for each window when computing averages.
Percent change must be NULL when the previous window's average is 0 to avoid division by zero.
Rolling window selection (6 weeks) must be defined relative to the derived MAX week_end_date.

2026-01-27T05:59:51.334111Z.final_sql:
WITH
  max_week_date_cte AS (
    SELECT MAX(week_end_date) AS max_week_end_date
    FROM drug_sales
  ),
  week_ranks_cte AS (
    SELECT d.week_end_date,
           DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY d.week_end_date DESC) AS week_rank
    FROM (SELECT DISTINCT week_end_date FROM drug_sales) d
    JOIN max_week_date_cte m
      ON d.week_end_date <= m.max_week_end_date
  ),
  window_classification_cte AS (
    SELECT
      t.week_end_date,
      t.qty_sold_pu,
      CASE
        WHEN wr.week_rank BETWEEN 1 AND 4 THEN 'recent_4w'
        WHEN wr.week_rank BETWEEN 5 AND 6 THEN 'prev_2w'
      END AS window_flag
    FROM drug_sales t
    JOIN week_ranks_cte wr
      ON t.week_end_date = wr.week_end_date
    WHERE wr.week_rank <= 6
  ),
  weekly_sales_cte AS (
    SELECT
      window_flag,
      week_end_date,
      SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS weekly_total
    FROM window_classification_cte
    WHERE window_flag IN ('recent_4w', 'prev_2w')
    GROUP BY window_flag, week_end_date
  ),
  window_weekly_avg_cte AS (
    SELECT
      window_flag,
      AVG(weekly_total) AS avg_weekly_qty,
      COUNT(*) AS weeks_in_window
    FROM weekly_sales_cte
    GROUP BY window_flag
  ),
  avg_growth_cte AS (
    SELECT
      SUM(CASE WHEN window_flag = 'recent_4w' THEN avg_weekly_qty END) AS recent_4w_avg_weekly_sales,
      SUM(CASE WHEN window_flag = 'prev_2w' THEN avg_weekly_qty END) AS previous_2w_avg_weekly_sales
    FROM window_weekly_avg_cte
  )
SELECT
  recent_4w_avg_weekly_sales,
  previous_2w_avg_weekly_sales,
  (recent_4w_avg_weekly_sales - previous_2w_avg_weekly_sales) AS absolute_change_avg,
  CASE
    WHEN previous_2w_avg_weekly_sales = 0 THEN NULL
    ELSE (recent_4w_avg_weekly_sales - previous_2w_avg_weekly_sales) / previous_2w_avg_weekly_sales
  END AS percent_change_avg
FROM avg_growth_cte;

DECISION: PASS
FEEDBACK:
SQL is safe and valid

2026-02-10T14:35:31.473474+00:00Z.question: what is the sales contribution of parent accounts which are academic, IDNs and Community?

query_decomposition.intent_summary: Compute the percentage contribution to national sales for parent account types Academic, IDN (including Other), and Community (including Unknown) over the most recent 52 weeks.

subqueries.name: max_date_cte
subqueries.purpose: Identify the most recent week_end_date in the dataset
subqueries.logic: Compute MAX(week_end_date) from drug_sales

subqueries.name: last_52_weeks_cte
subqueries.purpose: Limit data to the most recent 52 weeks relative to the max date
subqueries.logic: Select rows from drug_sales where week_end_date >= (MAX week_end_date from max_date_cte minus 51 weeks) to form a 52-week window

subqueries.name: mapped_types_cte
subqueries.purpose: Map parent_account_type values into three buckets per rules
subqueries.logic: Create a derived column mapped_parent_account_type with: CASE WHEN parent_account_type IN ('IDN/Hospital','Other') THEN 'IDN' WHEN parent_account_type IN ('Community','Unknown') THEN 'Community' WHEN parent_account_type = 'Academic' THEN 'Academic' END using rows from last_52_weeks_cte

subqueries.name: total_sales_cte
subqueries.purpose: Compute national total sales for the same 52-week window (denominator)
subqueries.logic: Sum qty_sold_pu over all rows from mapped_types_cte to produce total_sales_qty

subqueries.name: group_sales_cte
subqueries.purpose: Compute sales per mapped parent account type (numerators)
subqueries.logic: Group mapped_types_cte by mapped_parent_account_type and sum qty_sold_pu to produce group_sales_qty per bucket

subqueries.name: contribution_cte
subqueries.purpose: Calculate percentage contribution by mapped parent account type
subqueries.logic: Join group_sales_cte to total_sales_cte; for each bucket compute sales_contribution_percent = ROUND((group_sales_qty * 100.0) / NULLIF(total_sales_qty, 0)), with the result cast to integer

validation_rules: Default time period to the most recent 52 weeks using MAX(week_end_date) from the data; do not use system date.
Apply the rolling 52-week filter relative to the derived MAX(week_end_date).
Denominator for contribution is overall national sales within the same 52-week window.
When calculating metrics by parent account type: merge 'Other' into the IDN account type and merge 'Unknown' into the Community account type.
Output only three buckets labeled 'Academic', 'IDN', and 'Community' after mapping.
All metric outputs (contributions) must be rounded to zero decimal places and returned as integers representing percentages.

2026-02-10T14:35:31.473474+00:00Z.final_sql:
WITH
  max_date_cte AS (
    SELECT MAX(week_end_date) AS max_week_end_date
    FROM drug_sales
  ),
  last_52_weeks_cte AS (
    SELECT t.*
    FROM drug_sales t
    JOIN max_date_cte m ON 1=1
    WHERE t.week_end_date >= DATE_SUB(m.max_week_end_date, INTERVAL 51 WEEK)
  ),
  mapped_types_cte AS (
    SELECT
      CASE
        WHEN parent_account_type IN ('IDN/Hospital', 'Other') THEN 'IDN'
        WHEN parent_account_type IN ('Community', 'Unknown') THEN 'Community'
        WHEN parent_account_type = 'Academic' THEN 'Academic'
      END AS mapped_parent_account_type,
      qty_sold_pu
    FROM last_52_weeks_cte
  ),
  total_sales_cte AS (
    SELECT SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS total_sales_qty
    FROM mapped_types_cte
  ),
  group_sales_cte AS (
    SELECT
      mapped_parent_account_type,
      SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS group_sales_qty
    FROM mapped_types_cte
    GROUP BY mapped_parent_account_type
  ),
  contribution_cte AS (
    SELECT
      g.mapped_parent_account_type,
      CAST(ROUND((g.group_sales_qty * 100.0) / NULLIF(t.total_sales_qty, 0), 0) AS SIGNED) AS sales_contribution_percent
    FROM group_sales_cte g
    CROSS JOIN total_sales_cte t
  )
SELECT
  mapped_parent_account_type,
  sales_contribution_percent
FROM contribution_cte
ORDER BY sales_contribution_percent DESC;

2026-02-10T14:44:27.244663+00:00Z.question: what is the sales growth in recent 4 weeks by parent account type and how does that compare with nation?

query_decomposition.intent_summary: Compute sales growth (%) for the most recent 4 weeks by merged parent account type, and compare each to the national growth over the same period, using week_end_date anchored to the dataset's MAX week_end_date.

subqueries.name: max_date_cte
subqueries.purpose: Identify the most recent week_end_date in the dataset
subqueries.logic: Compute MAX(week_end_date) from drug_sales

subqueries.name: recent_weeks_cte
subqueries.purpose: Determine the 4 most recent distinct weeks anchored to the max week_end_date
subqueries.logic: Select the 4 most recent distinct week_end_date values where week_end_date <= (value from max_date_cte), ordered by week_end_date DESC, LIMIT 4

subqueries.name: previous_weeks_cte
subqueries.purpose: Determine the 4 distinct weeks immediately preceding the recent 4-week window
subqueries.logic: Find min_recent_week = MIN(week_end_date) from recent_weeks_cte; then select the 4 most recent distinct week_end_date values where week_end_date < min_recent_week, ordered by week_end_date DESC, LIMIT 4

subqueries.name: account_type_mapping_cte
subqueries.purpose: Create merged parent account type per business rules
subqueries.logic: From drug_sales, derive parent_account_type_merged as: CASE WHEN parent_account_type IN ('IDN/Hospital','Other') THEN 'IDN/Hospital' WHEN parent_account_type IN ('Community','Unknown') THEN 'Community' WHEN parent_account_type = 'Academic' THEN 'Academic' END; output week_end_date, qty_sold_pu, parent_account_type_merged

subqueries.name: sales_by_type_recent
subqueries.purpose: Aggregate recent 4-week sales by merged parent account type
subqueries.logic: From account_type_mapping_cte where week_end_date IN (recent_weeks_cte), group by parent_account_type_merged, compute SUM(qty_sold_pu) as recent_4wk_sales_by_type and also compute recent_week_count = COUNT(DISTINCT week_end_date) across the recent window (shared for all types)

subqueries.name: sales_by_type_previous
subqueries.purpose: Aggregate previous 4-week sales by merged parent account type
subqueries.logic: From account_type_mapping_cte where week_end_date IN (previous_weeks_cte), group by parent_account_type_merged, compute SUM(qty_sold_pu) as previous_4wk_sales_by_type and also compute previous_week_count = COUNT(DISTINCT week_end_date) across the previous window (shared for all types)

subqueries.name: national_recent
subqueries.purpose: Aggregate national recent 4-week sales and week count
subqueries.logic: From drug_sales where week_end_date IN (recent_weeks_cte), compute SUM(qty_sold_pu) as national_recent_4wk_sales and COUNT(DISTINCT week_end_date) as national_recent_week_count

subqueries.name: national_previous
subqueries.purpose: Aggregate national previous 4-week sales and week count
subqueries.logic: From drug_sales where week_end_date IN (previous_weeks_cte), compute SUM(qty_sold_pu) as national_previous_4wk_sales and COUNT(DISTINCT week_end_date) as national_previous_week_count

subqueries.name: final_growth_by_type
subqueries.purpose: Compute growth (%) by merged parent account type and compare to national growth (%) with required rounding and edge-case handling
subqueries.logic: Join sales_by_type_recent and sales_by_type_previous on parent_account_type_merged. Cross join national_recent and national_previous to attach national benchmarks. Let r_weeks = recent_week_count (from recent_weeks_cte) and p_weeks = previous_week_count (from previous_weeks_cte). Compute normalized recent and previous averages: type_recent_avg = recent_4wk_sales_by_type / NULLIF(r_weeks,0); type_previous_avg = previous_4wk_sales_by_type / NULLIF(p_weeks,0). Compute type_growth_pct as: if type_previous_avg = 0 and type_recent_avg > 0 then 100, else if type_previous_avg = 0 and type_recent_avg = 0 then 0, else ((type_recent_avg - type_previous_avg) / type_previous_avg) * 100. Similarly compute national_recent_avg = national_recent_4wk_sales / NULLIF(national_recent_week_count,0); national_previous_avg = national_previous_4wk_sales / NULLIF(national_previous_week_count,0); national_growth_pct as: if national_previous_avg = 0 and national_recent_avg > 0 then 100, else if national_previous_avg = 0 and national_recent_avg = 0 then 0, else ((national_recent_avg - national_previous_avg) / national_previous_avg) * 100. Finally compute variance_to_national_pct = type_growth_pct - national_growth_pct. Round all percentage outputs to 0 decimals and cast to integers.

validation_rules: Rolling windows must be relative to MAX(week_end_date) from the data; do not use system date.
Define two contiguous windows of equal intended length: the most recent 4 distinct weeks and the 4 distinct weeks immediately prior.
If either window has fewer than 4 distinct weeks, normalize sales to weekly averages (divide totals by distinct week counts) before computing growth.
When calculating metrics by parent account type: merge 'Other' into 'IDN/Hospital' and merge 'Unknown' into 'Community'.
All growth metrics must be expressed in percentage format, rounded to zero decimal places, and returned as integers.
For growth metrics, if the previous period value is 0 and the current period value is greater than 0, report 100%. If both are 0, report 0%.
Use week_end_date (not shipt_dt) for weekly period calculations.
Comparison to nation must use overall national sales as the benchmark for the same windows.

2026-02-10T14:44:27.244663+00:00Z.final_sql:
WITH
max_date_cte AS (
  SELECT MAX(week_end_date) AS max_week_end_date
  FROM drug_sales
),
recent_weeks_cte AS (
  SELECT week_end_date
  FROM (
    SELECT DISTINCT week_end_date
    FROM drug_sales
    WHERE week_end_date <= (SELECT max_week_end_date FROM max_date_cte)
    ORDER BY week_end_date DESC
    LIMIT 4
  ) rw
),
previous_weeks_cte AS (
  SELECT week_end_date
  FROM (
    SELECT DISTINCT week_end_date
    FROM drug_sales
    WHERE week_end_date < (SELECT MIN(week_end_date) FROM recent_weeks_cte)
    ORDER BY week_end_date DESC
    LIMIT 4
  ) pw
),
account_type_mapping_cte AS (
  SELECT
    week_end_date,
    qty_sold_pu,
    CASE
      WHEN parent_account_type IN ('IDN/Hospital','Other') THEN 'IDN/Hospital'
      WHEN parent_account_type IN ('Community','Unknown') THEN 'Community'
      WHEN parent_account_type = 'Academic' THEN 'Academic'
    END AS parent_account_type_merged
  FROM drug_sales
),
sales_by_type_recent AS (
  SELECT
    atm.parent_account_type_merged,
    SUM(atm.qty_sold_pu) AS recent_4wk_sales_by_type,
    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM recent_weeks_cte) AS recent_week_count
  FROM account_type_mapping_cte atm
  JOIN recent_weeks_cte rw ON atm.week_end_date = rw.week_end_date
  GROUP BY atm.parent_account_type_merged
),
sales_by_type_previous AS (
  SELECT
    atm.parent_account_type_merged,
    SUM(atm.qty_sold_pu) AS previous_4wk_sales_by_type,
    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM previous_weeks_cte) AS previous_week_count
  FROM account_type_mapping_cte atm
  JOIN previous_weeks_cte pw ON atm.week_end_date = pw.week_end_date
  GROUP BY atm.parent_account_type_merged
),
national_recent AS (
  SELECT
    SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS national_recent_4wk_sales,
    COUNT(DISTINCT week_end_date) AS national_recent_week_count
  FROM drug_sales
  WHERE week_end_date IN (SELECT week_end_date FROM recent_weeks_cte)
),
national_previous AS (
  SELECT
    SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS national_previous_4wk_sales,
    COUNT(DISTINCT week_end_date) AS national_previous_week_count
  FROM drug_sales
  WHERE week_end_date IN (SELECT week_end_date FROM previous_weeks_cte)
),
final_calc AS (
  SELECT
    sr.parent_account_type_merged,
    IFNULL(sr.recent_4wk_sales_by_type / NULLIF(sr.recent_week_count,0), 0) AS type_recent_avg,
    IFNULL(sp.previous_4wk_sales_by_type / NULLIF(sp.previous_week_count,0), 0) AS type_previous_avg,
    IFNULL(nr.national_recent_4wk_sales / NULLIF(nr.national_recent_week_count,0), 0) AS national_recent_avg,
    IFNULL(np.national_previous_4wk_sales / NULLIF(np.national_previous_week_count,0), 0) AS national_previous_avg
  FROM sales_by_type_recent sr
  JOIN sales_by_type_previous sp
    ON sr.parent_account_type_merged = sp.parent_account_type_merged
  CROSS JOIN national_recent nr
  CROSS JOIN national_previous np
)
SELECT
  parent_account_type_merged,
  CAST(ROUND(
    CASE
      WHEN type_previous_avg = 0 AND type_recent_avg > 0 THEN 100
      WHEN type_previous_avg = 0 AND type_recent_avg = 0 THEN 0
      ELSE ((type_recent_avg - type_previous_avg) / type_previous_avg) * 100
    END
  ,0) AS SIGNED) AS type_growth_pct,
  CAST(ROUND(
    CASE
      WHEN national_previous_avg = 0 AND national_recent_avg > 0 THEN 100
      WHEN national_previous_avg = 0 AND national_recent_avg = 0 THEN 0
      ELSE ((national_recent_avg - national_previous_avg) / national_previous_avg) * 100
    END
  ,0) AS SIGNED) AS national_growth_pct,
  CAST(ROUND(
    (
      CASE
        WHEN type_previous_avg = 0 AND type_recent_avg > 0 THEN 100
        WHEN type_previous_avg = 0 AND type_recent_avg = 0 THEN 0
        ELSE ((type_recent_avg - type_previous_avg) / type_previous_avg) * 100
      END
    ) -
    (
      CASE
        WHEN national_previous_avg = 0 AND national_recent_avg > 0 THEN 100
        WHEN national_previous_avg = 0 AND national_recent_avg = 0 THEN 0
        ELSE ((national_recent_avg - national_previous_avg) / national_previous_avg) * 100
      END
    )
  ,0) AS SIGNED) AS variance_to_national_pct
FROM final_calc
ORDER BY parent_account_type_merged ASC;

2026-02-10T14:58:59.584893+00:00Z.question: what is the sales growth in recent 4 weeks compared to previous 13 weeks by parent account type and how does that compare with nation?

query_decomposition.intent_summary: Compute sales growth as a percentage for recent 4 weeks versus the immediately preceding 13 weeks, grouped by merged parent account type, normalized to weekly averages due to unequal period lengths, and compare each group's growth to the national growth for the same periods.

subqueries.name: max_date_cte
subqueries.purpose: Identify the most recent week_end_date in the dataset.
subqueries.logic: Select MAX(week_end_date) as max_week_end_date from drug_sales.

subqueries.name: recent_weeks_cte
subqueries.purpose: Determine the set of the most recent 4 distinct weeks and their count.
subqueries.logic: From drug_sales, select DISTINCT week_end_date where week_end_date <= (select max_week_end_date from max_date_cte), order by week_end_date DESC limit 4. Expose: all 4 week_end_date values as recent_weeks and COUNT(*) as recent_weeks_n.

subqueries.name: prev_weeks_cte
subqueries.purpose: Determine the set of the 13 distinct weeks immediately preceding the recent 4-week window and their count.
subqueries.logic: Find min_recent_week = MIN(week_end_date) from recent_weeks_cte. From drug_sales, select DISTINCT week_end_date where week_end_date < min_recent_week, order by week_end_date DESC limit 13. Expose: all 13 week_end_date values as prev_weeks and COUNT(*) as prev_weeks_n.

subqueries.name: parent_type_merged_cte
subqueries.purpose: Normalize parent_account_type categories per business rule.
subqueries.logic: Select week_end_date, qty_sold_pu, CASE WHEN parent_account_type = 'Other' THEN 'IDN/Hospital' WHEN parent_account_type = 'Unknown' THEN 'Community' ELSE parent_account_type END as parent_account_type_merged from drug_sales.

subqueries.name: recent_sales_by_type_cte
subqueries.purpose: Aggregate recent 4-week sales by merged parent account type.
subqueries.logic: Join parent_type_merged_cte to recent_weeks_cte on week_end_date. Group by parent_account_type_merged and compute SUM(qty_sold_pu) as recent_4w_sales_total_by_type.

subqueries.name: prev_sales_by_type_cte
subqueries.purpose: Aggregate previous 13-week sales by merged parent account type.
subqueries.logic: Join parent_type_merged_cte to prev_weeks_cte on week_end_date. Group by parent_account_type_merged and compute SUM(qty_sold_pu) as prev_13w_sales_total_by_type.

subqueries.name: national_recent_sales_cte
subqueries.purpose: Aggregate national recent 4-week sales total and weeks count.
subqueries.logic: Filter parent_type_merged_cte to week_end_date in recent_weeks_cte. Compute SUM(qty_sold_pu) as national_recent_4w_sales_total. Also select (select recent_weeks_n from recent_weeks_cte) as recent_weeks_n.

subqueries.name: national_prev_sales_cte
subqueries.purpose: Aggregate national previous 13-week sales total and weeks count.
subqueries.logic: Filter parent_type_merged_cte to week_end_date in prev_weeks_cte. Compute SUM(qty_sold_pu) as national_prev_13w_sales_total. Also select (select prev_weeks_n from prev_weeks_cte) as prev_weeks_n.

subqueries.name: weekly_avgs_and_growth_by_type_cte
subqueries.purpose: Compute weekly averages for each period and growth percentage by merged parent account type.
subqueries.logic: Left join recent_sales_by_type_cte and prev_sales_by_type_cte on parent_account_type_merged. Bring in recent_weeks_n and prev_weeks_n from recent_weeks_cte and prev_weeks_cte (as scalars). Compute recent_avg_per_week_by_type = COALESCE(recent_4w_sales_total_by_type, 0) / NULLIF(recent_weeks_n, 0). Compute prev_avg_per_week_by_type = COALESCE(prev_13w_sales_total_by_type, 0) / NULLIF(prev_weeks_n, 0). Compute sales_growth_pct_by_type with zero-handling: IF prev_avg_per_week_by_type = 0 AND recent_avg_per_week_by_type > 0 THEN 100 ELSE IF prev_avg_per_week_by_type = 0 AND recent_avg_per_week_by_type = 0 THEN 0 ELSE ((recent_avg_per_week_by_type - prev_avg_per_week_by_type) / prev_avg_per_week_by_type) * 100. Round sales_growth_pct_by_type to 0 decimals.

subqueries.name: national_weekly_avgs_and_growth_cte
subqueries.purpose: Compute national weekly averages and growth percentage.
subqueries.logic: From national_recent_sales_cte and national_prev_sales_cte, compute national_recent_avg_per_week = national_recent_4w_sales_total / NULLIF(recent_weeks_n, 0) and national_prev_avg_per_week = national_prev_13w_sales_total / NULLIF(prev_weeks_n, 0). Compute national_sales_growth_pct with zero-handling: IF national_prev_avg_per_week = 0 AND national_recent_avg_per_week > 0 THEN 100 ELSE IF national_prev_avg_per_week = 0 AND national_recent_avg_per_week = 0 THEN 0 ELSE ((national_recent_avg_per_week - national_prev_avg_per_week) / national_prev_avg_per_week) * 100. Round national_sales_growth_pct to 0 decimals.

subqueries.name: final_output_cte
subqueries.purpose: Attach national comparator to each parent account type row and compute variance vs nation.
subqueries.logic: Cross join weekly_avgs_and_growth_by_type_cte with national_weekly_avgs_and_growth_cte to add national metrics to each row. Compute variance_vs_national_pct_points = sales_growth_pct_by_type - national_sales_growth_pct. Round recent_avg_per_week_by_type and prev_avg_per_week_by_type to 0 decimals for output.

validation_rules: All rolling windows must be computed relative to MAX(week_end_date) from the data; never use system date.
The previous 13-week window must be the 13 distinct weeks immediately preceding the earliest week in the recent 4-week window.
Because periods differ in length (4 vs 13 weeks), normalize sales to weekly averages using the number of distinct weeks in each window computed globally, not per account type.
When calculating growth: if previous period average equals 0 and current period average is greater than 0, report growth as 100%; if both are 0, report growth as 0%.
Merge parent_account_type values: map 'Other' into 'IDN/Hospital' and map 'Unknown' into 'Community' before grouping.
All growth metrics must be expressed in percentage format, rounded to zero decimal places and returned as integers.
Recent and previous weekly average outputs must also be rounded to zero decimal places and returned as integers.
Compute national growth across all records using the same windows and normalization, and include variance_vs_national_pct_points = group_growth_pct - national_growth_pct.

If fewer than 17 distinct weeks exist from the max week_end_date, use all available weeks: recent window up to 4 most-recent distinct weeks, previous window up to 13 distinct weeks immediately prior; continue normalization using the actual distinct week counts.

2026-02-10T14:58:59.584893+00:00Z.final_sql:
WITH
max_date_cte AS (
  SELECT MAX(week_end_date) AS max_week_end_date
  FROM drug_sales
),
recent_weeks_cte AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT week_end_date
  FROM drug_sales
  WHERE week_end_date <= (SELECT max_week_end_date FROM max_date_cte)
  ORDER BY week_end_date DESC
  LIMIT 4
),
prev_weeks_cte AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT ts.week_end_date
  FROM drug_sales ts
  WHERE ts.week_end_date < (SELECT MIN(week_end_date) FROM recent_weeks_cte)
  ORDER BY ts.week_end_date DESC
  LIMIT 13
),
parent_type_merged_cte AS (
  SELECT
    week_end_date,
    qty_sold_pu,
    CASE
      WHEN parent_account_type = 'Other' THEN 'IDN/Hospital'
      WHEN parent_account_type = 'Unknown' THEN 'Community'
      ELSE parent_account_type
    END AS parent_account_type_merged
  FROM drug_sales
),
recent_sales_by_type_cte AS (
  SELECT
    p.parent_account_type_merged,
    SUM(p.qty_sold_pu) AS recent_4w_sales_total_by_type
  FROM parent_type_merged_cte p
  INNER JOIN recent_weeks_cte r
    ON p.week_end_date = r.week_end_date
  GROUP BY p.parent_account_type_merged
),
prev_sales_by_type_cte AS (
  SELECT
    p.parent_account_type_merged,
    SUM(p.qty_sold_pu) AS prev_13w_sales_total_by_type
  FROM parent_type_merged_cte p
  INNER JOIN prev_weeks_cte pw
    ON p.week_end_date = pw.week_end_date
  GROUP BY p.parent_account_type_merged
),
national_recent_sales_cte AS (
  SELECT
    SUM(p.qty_sold_pu) AS national_recent_4w_sales_total,
    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM recent_weeks_cte) AS recent_weeks_n
  FROM parent_type_merged_cte p
  INNER JOIN recent_weeks_cte r
    ON p.week_end_date = r.week_end_date
),
national_prev_sales_cte AS (
  SELECT
    SUM(p.qty_sold_pu) AS national_prev_13w_sales_total,
    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM prev_weeks_cte) AS prev_weeks_n
  FROM parent_type_merged_cte p
  INNER JOIN prev_weeks_cte pw
    ON p.week_end_date = pw.week_end_date
),
weekly_avgs_by_type_cte AS (
  SELECT
    r.parent_account_type_merged,
    COALESCE(r.recent_4w_sales_total_by_type, 0) / NULLIF((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM recent_weeks_cte), 0) AS recent_avg_per_week_by_type,
    COALESCE(pv.prev_13w_sales_total_by_type, 0) / NULLIF((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM prev_weeks_cte), 0) AS prev_avg_per_week_by_type
  FROM recent_sales_by_type_cte r
  LEFT JOIN prev_sales_by_type_cte pv
    ON r.parent_account_type_merged = pv.parent_account_type_merged
),
weekly_avgs_and_growth_by_type_cte AS (
  SELECT
    w.parent_account_type_merged,
    w.recent_avg_per_week_by_type,
    w.prev_avg_per_week_by_type,
    CASE
      WHEN COALESCE(w.prev_avg_per_week_by_type, 0) = 0 AND COALESCE(w.recent_avg_per_week_by_type, 0) > 0 THEN 100
      WHEN COALESCE(w.prev_avg_per_week_by_type, 0) = 0 AND COALESCE(w.recent_avg_per_week_by_type, 0) = 0 THEN 0
      ELSE ((w.recent_avg_per_week_by_type - w.prev_avg_per_week_by_type) / w.prev_avg_per_week_by_type) * 100
    END AS sales_growth_pct_by_type
  FROM weekly_avgs_by_type_cte w
),
national_weekly_avgs_cte AS (
  SELECT
    nr.national_recent_4w_sales_total / NULLIF(nr.recent_weeks_n, 0) AS national_recent_avg_per_week,
    np.national_prev_13w_sales_total / NULLIF(np.prev_weeks_n, 0) AS national_prev_avg_per_week
  FROM national_recent_sales_cte nr
  CROSS JOIN national_prev_sales_cte np
),
national_weekly_avgs_and_growth_cte AS (
  SELECT
    n.national_recent_avg_per_week,
    n.national_prev_avg_per_week,
    CASE
      WHEN COALESCE(n.national_prev_avg_per_week, 0) = 0 AND COALESCE(n.national_recent_avg_per_week, 0) > 0 THEN 100
      WHEN COALESCE(n.national_prev_avg_per_week, 0) = 0 AND COALESCE(n.national_recent_avg_per_week, 0) = 0 THEN 0
      ELSE ((n.national_recent_avg_per_week - n.national_prev_avg_per_week) / n.national_prev_avg_per_week) * 100
    END AS national_sales_growth_pct
  FROM national_weekly_avgs_cte n
),
final_output_cte AS (
  SELECT
    w.parent_account_type_merged,
    CAST(ROUND(w.recent_avg_per_week_by_type, 0) AS SIGNED) AS recent_4w_avg_units_per_week,
    CAST(ROUND(w.prev_avg_per_week_by_type, 0) AS SIGNED) AS prev_13w_avg_units_per_week,
    CAST(ROUND(w.sales_growth_pct_by_type, 0) AS SIGNED) AS sales_growth_pct,
    CAST(ROUND(n.national_sales_growth_pct, 0) AS SIGNED) AS national_sales_growth_pct,
    CAST(ROUND(w.sales_growth_pct_by_type - n.national_sales_growth_pct, 0) AS SIGNED) AS variance_vs_national_pct_points
  FROM weekly_avgs_and_growth_by_type_cte w
  CROSS JOIN national_weekly_avgs_and_growth_cte n
)
SELECT
  parent_account_type_merged,
  recent_4w_avg_units_per_week,
  prev_13w_avg_units_per_week,
  sales_growth_pct,
  national_sales_growth_pct,
  variance_vs_national_pct_points
FROM final_output_cte

2026-02-11T10:21:09.533692+00:00Z.question: what is the sales contribution of parent accounts by BC potential segment

query_decomposition.intent_summary: Compute sales contribution by parent BC potential segment over the most recent 52 weeks, using national total sales as the denominator.

subqueries.name: max_date_cte
subqueries.purpose: Identify the most recent week_end_date in the dataset
subqueries.logic: Compute MAX(week_end_date) from drug_sales

subqueries.name: rolling_window_start_cte
subqueries.purpose: Determine the start date for the latest 52-week window
subqueries.logic: Calculate start_date as (MAX(week_end_date) - 51 weeks) to create a 52-week inclusive window

subqueries.name: filtered_sales_cte
subqueries.purpose: Restrict data to the last 52 weeks based on derived dates
subqueries.logic: Select rows from drug_sales where week_end_date BETWEEN start_date AND max_date from the above CTEs

subqueries.name: total_sales_cte
subqueries.purpose: Compute the national total sales for the denominator within the same 52-week window
subqueries.logic: From filtered_sales_cte, compute SUM(qty_sold_pu) as total_sales

subqueries.name: segment_sales_cte
subqueries.purpose: Compute sales totals by parent BC potential segment within the 52-week window
subqueries.logic: From filtered_sales_cte, GROUP BY parent_bc_segment and compute SUM(qty_sold_pu) as segment_sales

subqueries.name: contribution_calc
subqueries.purpose: Calculate each BC potential segment's sales contribution to national sales
subqueries.logic: Cross join segment_sales_cte with total_sales_cte and compute contribution_pct = ROUND(100 * segment_sales / NULLIF(total_sales, 0)) returning an integer

validation_rules: Default to most recent 52 weeks; the rolling window must be relative to MAX(week_end_date) in the data and inclusive of both endpoints.
Do not use system date; derive MAX(week_end_date) from the table.
Denominator for contribution is overall national total sales within the same 52-week window.
Return all metric outputs as integers; contribution_pct as a whole number percentage rounded to zero decimals.
If total_sales equals 0, set contribution_pct to 0 to avoid division by zero.
Ensure one output row per unique parent_bc_segment.
Use only allowed values for parent_bc_segment: 'High', 'Medium', 'Low', 'Unknown'.
No time period comparisons are performed; weekly normalization is not required.

2026-02-11T10:21:09.533692+00:00Z.final_sql:
WITH
  max_date_cte AS (
    SELECT MAX(week_end_date) AS max_date
    FROM drug_sales
  ),
  rolling_window_start_cte AS (
    SELECT DATE_SUB(max_date, INTERVAL 51 WEEK) AS start_date
    FROM max_date_cte
  ),
  filtered_sales_cte AS (
    SELECT ts.*
    FROM drug_sales ts
    CROSS JOIN max_date_cte md
    CROSS JOIN rolling_window_start_cte rs
    WHERE ts.week_end_date BETWEEN rs.start_date AND md.max_date
  ),
  total_sales_cte AS (
    SELECT SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS total_sales
    FROM filtered_sales_cte
  ),
  segment_sales_cte AS (
    SELECT parent_bc_segment, SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS segment_sales
    FROM filtered_sales_cte
    WHERE parent_bc_segment IN ('High', 'Medium', 'Low', 'Unknown')
    GROUP BY parent_bc_segment
  ),
  contribution_calc AS (
    SELECT
      s.parent_bc_segment,
      CAST(s.segment_sales AS SIGNED) AS segment_sales,
      CAST(ROUND(CASE WHEN t.total_sales IS NULL OR t.total_sales = 0 THEN 0 ELSE (100.0 * s.segment_sales) / t.total_sales END, 0) AS SIGNED) AS contribution_pct
    FROM segment_sales_cte s
    CROSS JOIN total_sales_cte t
  )
SELECT parent_bc_segment, segment_sales, contribution_pct
FROM contribution_calc
ORDER BY contribution_pct DESC, parent_bc_segment ASC;

2026-02-11T10:28:47.950403+00:00Z.question: what is the sales growth in recent 4 weeks by BC potential segment and how does that compare with nation

query_decomposition.intent_summary: Compute sales growth percentage for the most recent 4 weeks by parent_bc_segment and compare each segment's growth to the overall national growth over the same periods.

subqueries.name: max_date_cte
subqueries.purpose: Identify the most recent week_end_date in the dataset
subqueries.logic: Compute MAX(week_end_date) from drug_sales

subqueries.name: last_8_weeks_cte
subqueries.purpose: Define the last 8 distinct weeks and label two 4-week windows
subqueries.logic: Select DISTINCT week_end_date from drug_sales; order by week_end_date DESC; assign row_number (or dense_rank) as week_rank; keep week_rank 1 through 8; set window_label = 'current' for ranks 1-4 and 'previous' for ranks 5-8

subqueries.name: window_week_counts_cte
subqueries.purpose: Get the number of distinct weeks in each window for normalization
subqueries.logic: From last_8_weeks_cte, group by window_label and compute weeks_in_window = COUNT(DISTINCT week_end_date)

subqueries.name: weekly_flagged_sales_cte
subqueries.purpose: Attach window labels to transaction rows limited to the last 8 weeks
subqueries.logic: Join drug_sales to last_8_weeks_cte on week_end_date; retain parent_bc_segment, qty_sold_pu, and window_label

subqueries.name: segment_window_agg_cte
subqueries.purpose: Aggregate sales per BC potential segment by window
subqueries.logic: Group weekly_flagged_sales_cte by parent_bc_segment and window_label; compute sales_units = SUM(qty_sold_pu)

subqueries.name: national_window_agg_cte
subqueries.purpose: Aggregate national sales totals by window
subqueries.logic: Group weekly_flagged_sales_cte by window_label; compute sales_units_national = SUM(qty_sold_pu)

subqueries.name: segment_pivot_cte
subqueries.purpose: Create current and previous 4-week totals per segment
subqueries.logic: Pivot segment_window_agg_cte so each parent_bc_segment has current_sales_4w (window_label='current') and previous_sales_4w (window_label='previous'); join window_week_counts_cte to attach current_weeks_count and previous_weeks_count

subqueries.name: national_pivot_cte
subqueries.purpose: Create single-row current and previous national totals with week counts
subqueries.logic: Pivot national_window_agg_cte to national_current_sales_4w and national_previous_sales_4w based on window_label; join window_week_counts_cte to attach current_weeks_count and previous_weeks_count

subqueries.name: final_metrics_cte
subqueries.purpose: Calculate growth percentages and compare each segment to national
subqueries.logic: For each parent_bc_segment row from segment_pivot_cte, compute normalized averages: seg_current_avg = current_sales_4w / NULLIF(current_weeks_count,0); seg_previous_avg = previous_sales_4w / NULLIF(previous_weeks_count,0). Compute growth_pct as: CASE WHEN seg_previous_avg = 0 AND seg_current_avg > 0 THEN 100 WHEN seg_previous_avg = 0 AND seg_current_avg = 0 THEN 0 ELSE ROUND(((seg_current_avg - seg_previous_avg) / seg_previous_avg) * 100, 0) END. Cross join national_pivot_cte and compute nat_current_avg = national_current_sales_4w / NULLIF(current_weeks_count,0); nat_previous_avg = national_previous_sales_4w / NULLIF(previous_weeks_count,0); national_growth_pct using the same CASE logic; growth_vs_national_pct = growth_pct - national_growth_pct. Ensure all percentage outputs are integers (rounded to 0 decimals).

validation_rules: Rolling window must be computed relative to MAX(week_end_date); do not use system date
Use exactly the 8 most recent distinct week_end_date values to form two 4-week windows: ranks 1-4 = current, 5-8 = previous
If fewer than 8 distinct weeks exist, use the available distinct weeks in each window and normalize by dividing totals by the number of weeks in that window before computing growth

Growth formula: ((current_avg_per_week - previous_avg_per_week) / previous_avg_per_week) * 100; if previous_avg_per_week = 0 and current_avg_per_week > 0, growth = 100%; if both are 0, growth = 0%; if current_avg_per_week = 0 and previous_avg_per_week > 0, growth = -100%
All growth metrics must be expressed as percentages and rounded to zero decimal places (integers)
Include all parent_bc_segment values including 'Unknown'; do not exclude any segments
Use week_end_date for all time alignment and windowing

2026-02-11T10:28:47.950403+00:00Z.final_sql:
WITH
max_date_cte AS (
  SELECT MAX(week_end_date) AS max_week_end_date
  FROM drug_sales
),
last_8_weeks_cte AS (
  SELECT
    d.week_end_date,
    d.week_rank,
    CASE
      WHEN d.week_rank BETWEEN 1 AND 4 THEN 'current'
      WHEN d.week_rank BETWEEN 5 AND 8 THEN 'previous'
    END AS window_label
  FROM (
    SELECT
      DISTINCT s.week_end_date,
      DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY s.week_end_date DESC) AS week_rank
    FROM drug_sales s
    WHERE s.week_end_date <= (SELECT max_week_end_date FROM max_date_cte)
  ) d
  WHERE d.week_rank BETWEEN 1 AND 8
),
window_week_counts_cte AS (
  SELECT
    window_label,
    COUNT(DISTINCT week_end_date) AS weeks_in_window
  FROM last_8_weeks_cte
  GROUP BY window_label
),
weekly_flagged_sales_cte AS (
  SELECT
    s.parent_bc_segment,
    s.qty_sold_pu,
    l.window_label,
    s.week_end_date
  FROM drug_sales s
  JOIN last_8_weeks_cte l
    ON s.week_end_date = l.week_end_date
  WHERE s.week_end_date >= (SELECT MIN(week_end_date) FROM last_8_weeks_cte)
),
segment_window_agg_cte AS (
  SELECT
    parent_bc_segment,
    window_label,
    SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS sales_units
  FROM weekly_flagged_sales_cte
  GROUP BY parent_bc_segment, window_label
),
national_window_agg_cte AS (
  SELECT
    window_label,
    SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS sales_units_national
  FROM weekly_flagged_sales_cte
  GROUP BY window_label
),
counts_pivot_cte AS (
  SELECT
    SUM(CASE WHEN window_label = 'current' THEN weeks_in_window ELSE 0 END) AS current_weeks_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN window_label = 'previous' THEN weeks_in_window ELSE 0 END) AS previous_weeks_count
  FROM window_week_counts_cte
),
segment_pivot_cte AS (
  SELECT
    s.parent_bc_segment,
    SUM(CASE WHEN s.window_label = 'current' THEN s.sales_units ELSE 0 END) AS current_sales_4w,
    SUM(CASE WHEN s.window_label = 'previous' THEN s.sales_units ELSE 0 END) AS previous_sales_4w
  FROM segment_window_agg_cte s
  GROUP BY s.parent_bc_segment
),
national_pivot_cte AS (
  SELECT
    SUM(CASE WHEN window_label = 'current' THEN sales_units_national ELSE 0 END) AS national_current_sales_4w,
    SUM(CASE WHEN window_label = 'previous' THEN sales_units_national ELSE 0 END) AS national_previous_sales_4w
  FROM national_window_agg_cte
),
final_metrics_cte AS (
  SELECT
    q.parent_bc_segment,
    q.current_sales_4w,
    q.previous_sales_4w,
    q.national_current_sales_4w,
    q.national_previous_sales_4w,
    CASE
      WHEN q.seg_previous_avg = 0 AND q.seg_current_avg > 0 THEN 100
      WHEN q.seg_previous_avg = 0 AND q.seg_current_avg = 0 THEN 0
      WHEN q.seg_current_avg = 0 AND q.seg_previous_avg > 0 THEN -100
      ELSE ROUND(((q.seg_current_avg - q.seg_previous_avg) / q.seg_previous_avg) * 100, 0)
    END AS growth_pct,
    CASE
      WHEN q.nat_previous_avg = 0 AND q.nat_current_avg > 0 THEN 100
      WHEN q.nat_previous_avg = 0 AND q.nat_current_avg = 0 THEN 0
      WHEN q.nat_current_avg = 0 AND q.nat_previous_avg > 0 THEN -100
      ELSE ROUND(((q.nat_current_avg - q.nat_previous_avg) / q.nat_previous_avg) * 100, 0)
    END AS national_growth_pct
  FROM (
    SELECT
      sp.parent_bc_segment,
      sp.current_sales_4w,
      sp.previous_sales_4w,
      np.national_current_sales_4w,
      np.national_previous_sales_4w,
      cp.current_weeks_count,
      cp.previous_weeks_count,
      CASE WHEN cp.current_weeks_count = 0 THEN 0 ELSE sp.current_sales_4w / cp.current_weeks_count END AS seg_current_avg,
      CASE WHEN cp.previous_weeks_count = 0 THEN 0 ELSE sp.previous_sales_4w / cp.previous_weeks_count END AS seg_previous_avg,
      CASE WHEN cp.current_weeks_count = 0 THEN 0 ELSE np.national_current_sales_4w / cp.current_weeks_count END AS nat_current_avg,
      CASE WHEN cp.previous_weeks_count = 0 THEN 0 ELSE np.national_previous_sales_4w / cp.previous_weeks_count END AS nat_previous_avg
    FROM segment_pivot_cte sp
    CROSS JOIN national_pivot_cte np
    CROSS JOIN counts_pivot_cte cp
  ) q
)
SELECT
  f.parent_bc_segment,
  f.current_sales_4w,
  f.previous_sales_4w,
  f.growth_pct,
  f.national_current_sales_4w,
  f.national_previous_sales_4w,
  f.national_growth_pct,
  (f.growth_pct - f.national_growth_pct) AS growth_vs_national_pct
FROM final_metrics_cte f
ORDER BY f.parent_bc_segment ASC;

query_decomposition.intent_summary: Compute sales growth by parent_bc_segment for the most recent 4 weeks versus the immediately preceding 5 weeks, normalize to weekly averages due to unequal period lengths, compare each segment's growth to national growth, and include the weekly average values for both periods at both the segment and national levels in the output.

subqueries.name: max_week_cte
subqueries.purpose: Identify the most recent week_end_date in the dataset
subqueries.logic: Select MAX(week_end_date) as max_week_end_date from drug_sales

subqueries.name: distinct_weeks_cte
subqueries.purpose: List all distinct week_end_date values up to and including the max week for ordered selection
subqueries.logic: From drug_sales, select DISTINCT week_end_date where week_end_date <= max_week_end_date (from max_week_cte), ordered by week_end_date DESC

subqueries.name: recent_4_weeks_cte
subqueries.purpose: Derive the set of the most recent 4 distinct weeks
subqueries.logic: Select the top 4 week_end_date values from distinct_weeks_cte ordered by week_end_date DESC

subqueries.name: previous_5_weeks_cte
subqueries.purpose: Derive the set of the 5 distinct weeks immediately preceding the most recent 4 weeks
subqueries.logic: Find the minimum week_end_date from recent_4_weeks_cte as recent4_min_date; from distinct_weeks_cte select the top 5 week_end_date values where week_end_date < recent4_min_date ordered by week_end_date DESC

subqueries.name: segment_period_sums_cte
subqueries.purpose: Compute segment-level sales sums for each period
subqueries.logic: From drug_sales group by parent_bc_segment; compute two conditional sums: recent_4w_sales_by_segment = SUM(qty_sold_pu) where week_end_date IN (recent_4_weeks_cte); prev_5w_sales_by_segment = SUM(qty_sold_pu) where week_end_date IN (previous_5_weeks_cte). Include all parent_bc_segment values including 'Unknown'.

subqueries.name: national_period_sums_cte
subqueries.purpose: Compute national-level sales sums for each period
subqueries.logic: From drug_sales (no grouping), compute two conditional sums across all rows: recent_4w_sales_national = SUM(qty_sold_pu) where week_end_date IN (recent_4_weeks_cte); prev_5w_sales_national = SUM(qty_sold_pu) where week_end_date IN (previous_5_weeks_cte).

subqueries.name: segment_weekly_avgs_cte
subqueries.purpose: Normalize segment-level sums to weekly averages due to unequal period lengths (no rounding here to preserve precision for growth calculation)
subqueries.logic: For each parent_bc_segment from segment_period_sums_cte, compute segment_current_weekly_avg_raw = recent_4w_sales_by_segment / 4.0 and segment_previous_weekly_avg_raw = prev_5w_sales_by_segment / 5.0.

subqueries.name: national_weekly_avgs_cte
subqueries.purpose: Normalize national-level sums to weekly averages due to unequal period lengths (no rounding here to preserve precision for growth calculation)
subqueries.logic: From national_period_sums_cte, compute national_current_weekly_avg_raw = recent_4w_sales_national / 4.0 and national_previous_weekly_avg_raw = prev_5w_sales_national / 5.0.

subqueries.name: national_growth_cte
subqueries.purpose: Compute national growth percentage with zero-handling
subqueries.logic: Compute national_sales_growth_pct_raw as: CASE WHEN national_previous_weekly_avg_raw = 0 AND national_current_weekly_avg_raw > 0 THEN 100 WHEN national_previous_weekly_avg_raw = 0 AND national_current_weekly_avg_raw = 0 THEN 0 ELSE ((national_current_weekly_avg_raw - national_previous_weekly_avg_raw) / national_previous_weekly_avg_raw) * 100 END.

subqueries.name: segment_growth_cte
subqueries.purpose: Compute segment growth percentage with zero-handling
subqueries.logic: For each parent_bc_segment from segment_weekly_avgs_cte, compute sales_growth_pct_raw as: CASE WHEN segment_previous_weekly_avg_raw = 0 AND segment_current_weekly_avg_raw > 0 THEN 100 WHEN segment_previous_weekly_avg_raw = 0 AND segment_current_weekly_avg_raw = 0 THEN 0 ELSE ((segment_current_weekly_avg_raw - segment_previous_weekly_avg_raw) / segment_previous_weekly_avg_raw) * 100 END.

subqueries.name: final_metrics_cte
subqueries.purpose: Attach national growth and national weekly averages to each segment, compute delta vs national, and round all outputs to integers
subqueries.logic: Cross-join segment_growth_cte with national_growth_cte and join national_weekly_avgs_cte to add national averages. Project: segment_previous_weekly_avg = ROUND(segment_previous_weekly_avg_raw, 0); segment_current_weekly_avg = ROUND(segment_current_weekly_avg_raw, 0); national_previous_weekly_avg = ROUND(national_previous_weekly_avg_raw, 0); national_current_weekly_avg = ROUND(national_current_weekly_avg_raw, 0); sales_growth_pct = ROUND(sales_growth_pct_raw, 0); national_sales_growth_pct = ROUND(national_sales_growth_pct_raw, 0); delta_vs_national_pct = sales_growth_pct - national_sales_growth_pct.

validation_rules: Most recent periods must be derived using MAX(week_end_date); do not use system date.
Define recent period as the 4 most recent distinct week_end_date values and previous period as the 5 distinct weeks immediately preceding the recent period.
Apply a 9-week base filter relative to MAX week_end_date to limit data used in period calculations.
Because period lengths differ (4 vs 5 weeks), normalize sums to weekly averages before computing growth.
Include integer-rounded weekly averages for both the recent and previous periods in the output at both the segment and national levels.
If previous-period weekly average is 0 and current-period weekly average is greater than 0, set growth to 100%.
If both previous and current weekly averages are 0, set growth to 0%.
Express all growth metrics in percentage format and round to 0 decimal places (integers).
When calculating metrics by parent account type, merge 'Other' into 'IDN/Hospital' and 'Unknown' into 'Community'; not applicable here as grouping is by parent_bc_segment.
Rolling window calculations must be relative to MAX(week_end_date) and applied after deriving the max date.

2026-02-11T10:41:45.644973+00:00Z.final_sql:
WITH
max_week_cte AS (
  SELECT MAX(week_end_date) AS max_week_end_date
  FROM drug_sales
),
distinct_weeks_cte AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT ts.week_end_date
  FROM drug_sales ts
  CROSS JOIN max_week_cte m
  WHERE ts.week_end_date >= DATE_SUB(m.max_week_end_date, INTERVAL 8 WEEK)
    AND ts.week_end_date <= m.max_week_end_date
),
recent_4_weeks_cte AS (
  SELECT d.week_end_date
  FROM distinct_weeks_cte d
  ORDER BY d.week_end_date DESC
  LIMIT 4
),
previous_5_weeks_cte AS (
  SELECT d.week_end_date
  FROM distinct_weeks_cte d
  WHERE d.week_end_date < (SELECT MIN(week_end_date) FROM recent_4_weeks_cte)
  ORDER BY d.week_end_date DESC
  LIMIT 5
),
segment_period_sums_cte AS (
  SELECT
    ts.parent_bc_segment,
    SUM(CASE WHEN ts.week_end_date IN (SELECT week_end_date FROM recent_4_weeks_cte) THEN ts.qty_sold_pu ELSE 0 END) AS recent_4w_sales_by_segment,
    SUM(CASE WHEN ts.week_end_date IN (SELECT week_end_date FROM previous_5_weeks_cte) THEN ts.qty_sold_pu ELSE 0 END) AS prev_5w_sales_by_segment
  FROM drug_sales ts
  CROSS JOIN max_week_cte m
  WHERE ts.week_end_date >= DATE_SUB(m.max_week_end_date, INTERVAL 8 WEEK)
    AND ts.week_end_date <= m.max_week_end_date
  GROUP BY ts.parent_bc_segment
),
national_period_sums_cte AS (
  SELECT
    SUM(CASE WHEN ts.week_end_date IN (SELECT week_end_date FROM recent_4_weeks_cte) THEN ts.qty_sold_pu ELSE 0 END) AS recent_4w_sales_national,
    SUM(CASE WHEN ts.week_end_date IN (SELECT week_end_date FROM previous_5_weeks_cte) THEN ts.qty_sold_pu ELSE 0 END) AS prev_5w_sales_national
  FROM drug_sales ts
  CROSS JOIN max_week_cte m
  WHERE ts.week_end_date >= DATE_SUB(m.max_week_end_date, INTERVAL 8 WEEK)
    AND ts.week_end_date <= m.max_week_end_date
),
segment_weekly_avgs_cte AS (
  SELECT
    s.parent_bc_segment,
    (s.recent_4w_sales_by_segment / 4.0) AS segment_current_weekly_avg_raw,
    (s.prev_5w_sales_by_segment / 5.0) AS segment_previous_weekly_avg_raw
  FROM segment_period_sums_cte s
),
national_weekly_avgs_cte AS (
  SELECT
    (n.recent_4w_sales_national / 4.0) AS national_current_weekly_avg_raw,
    (n.prev_5w_sales_national / 5.0) AS national_previous_weekly_avg_raw
  FROM national_period_sums_cte n
),
national_growth_cte AS (
  SELECT
    CASE
      WHEN nwa.national_previous_weekly_avg_raw = 0 AND nwa.national_current_weekly_avg_raw > 0 THEN 100
      WHEN nwa.national_previous_weekly_avg_raw = 0 AND nwa.national_current_weekly_avg_raw = 0 THEN 0
      ELSE ((nwa.national_current_weekly_avg_raw - nwa.national_previous_weekly_avg_raw) / nwa.national_previous_weekly_avg_raw) * 100
    END AS national_sales_growth_pct_raw
  FROM national_weekly_avgs_cte nwa
),
segment_growth_cte AS (
  SELECT
    swa.parent_bc_segment,
    swa.segment_current_weekly_avg_raw,
    swa.segment_previous_weekly_avg_raw,
    CASE
      WHEN swa.segment_previous_weekly_avg_raw = 0 AND swa.segment_current_weekly_avg_raw > 0 THEN 100
      WHEN swa.segment_previous_weekly_avg_raw = 0 AND swa.segment_current_weekly_avg_raw = 0 THEN 0
      ELSE ((swa.segment_current_weekly_avg_raw - swa.segment_previous_weekly_avg_raw) / swa.segment_previous_weekly_avg_raw) * 100
    END AS sales_growth_pct_raw
  FROM segment_weekly_avgs_cte swa
),
final_metrics_cte AS (
  SELECT
    sg.parent_bc_segment,
    CAST(ROUND(sg.segment_previous_weekly_avg_raw, 0) AS SIGNED) AS segment_previous_weekly_avg,
    CAST(ROUND(sg.segment_current_weekly_avg_raw, 0) AS SIGNED) AS segment_current_weekly_avg,
    CAST(ROUND(sg.sales_growth_pct_raw, 0) AS SIGNED) AS sales_growth_pct,
    CAST(ROUND(nwa.national_previous_weekly_avg_raw, 0) AS SIGNED) AS national_previous_weekly_avg,
    CAST(ROUND(nwa.national_current_weekly_avg_raw, 0) AS SIGNED) AS national_current_weekly_avg,
    CAST(ROUND(ng.national_sales_growth_pct_raw, 0) AS SIGNED) AS national_sales_growth_pct,
    CAST(ROUND(sg.sales_growth_pct_raw, 0) - ROUND(ng.national_sales_growth_pct_raw, 0) AS SIGNED) AS delta_vs_national_pct
  FROM segment_growth_cte sg
  CROSS JOIN national_growth_cte ng
  CROSS JOIN national_weekly_avgs_cte nwa
)
SELECT

  f.national_current_weekly_avg,
  f.national_sales_growth_pct,
  f.delta_vs_national_pct
FROM final_metrics_cte f
ORDER BY f.parent_bc_segment ASC;

2026-02-11T10:47:07.469494+00:00Z.question: what is the sales growth in recent 4 weeks by parent account types and BC potential segments and how does that compare with nation

query_decomposition.intent_summary: Compute sales growth percentage for the most recent 4 weeks versus the prior 4 weeks, broken out by merged parent account types and parent BC potential segments, and compare each group's growth to the national growth.

subqueries.name: max_week_cte
subqueries.purpose: Identify the most recent week_end_date in the dataset
subqueries.logic: Compute MAX(week_end_date) from drug_sales

subqueries.name: ranked_weeks_cte
subqueries.purpose: Select the most recent 8 distinct weeks and label the most recent 4 as current and the previous 4 as previous
subqueries.logic: From distinct week_end_date values, ordered descending relative to max_week_cte, assign dense_rank where 1 is most recent. Keep ranks 1-8. Define period_flag = 'current' for ranks 1-4 and 'previous' for ranks 5-8.

subqueries.name: base_data_cte
subqueries.purpose: Limit rows to the 8 selected weeks and derive merged parent account type
subqueries.logic: Join drug_sales to ranked_weeks_cte on week_end_date. Derive parent_account_type_merged with mapping: if parent_account_type in ('IDN/Hospital','Other') then 'IDN/Hospital'; if parent_account_type = 'Unknown' then 'Community'; otherwise keep original value ('Academic' or 'Community'). Preserve parent_bc_segment and period_flag.

subqueries.name: grouped_sales_by_dim_period
subqueries.purpose: Aggregate sales by merged parent account type, BC segment, and period
subqueries.logic: Group base_data_cte by parent_account_type_merged, parent_bc_segment, period_flag and compute SUM(qty_sold_pu) as sales_qty.

subqueries.name: pivot_group_sales
subqueries.purpose: Create side-by-side current and previous 4-week sales per group
subqueries.logic: For each (parent_account_type_merged, parent_bc_segment), compute current_4wk_sales = SUM(sales_qty where period_flag='current') and previous_4wk_sales = SUM(sales_qty where period_flag='previous').

subqueries.name: group_growth_calc
subqueries.purpose: Calculate growth percentage for each group
subqueries.logic: For each group from pivot_group_sales, compute group_growth_pct = CASE WHEN previous_4wk_sales = 0 AND current_4wk_sales > 0 THEN 100 WHEN previous_4wk_sales = 0 AND current_4wk_sales = 0 THEN 0 ELSE ((current_4wk_sales - previous_4wk_sales) / previous_4wk_sales) * 100 END. Round to 0 decimals (integer).

subqueries.name: national_sales_by_period
subqueries.purpose: Compute national current and previous 4-week sales totals
subqueries.logic: From base_data_cte, aggregate SUM(qty_sold_pu) by period_flag across all rows (no dimensional grouping) to produce national_current_4wk_sales and national_previous_4wk_sales.

subqueries.name: national_growth_calc
subqueries.purpose: Calculate national growth percentage
subqueries.logic: Compute national_growth_pct using the same growth rule as groups: CASE WHEN national_previous_4wk_sales = 0 AND national_current_4wk_sales > 0 THEN 100 WHEN national_previous_4wk_sales = 0 AND national_current_4wk_sales = 0 THEN 0 ELSE ((national_current_4wk_sales - national_previous_4wk_sales) / national_previous_4wk_sales) * 100 END. Round to 0 decimals (integer).

subqueries.name: final_join
subqueries.purpose: Attach national benchmarks to each group and compute comparison
subqueries.logic: Cross join national_growth_calc (and its sales totals) to each row in group_growth_calc; compute growth_vs_national_pct_point_diff = group_growth_pct - national_growth_pct. Round to 0 decimals (integer).

validation_rules: Derive the most recent period using MAX(week_end_date); never use system date.
Define current period as the 4 most recent distinct week_end_date values and previous period as the 4 distinct weeks immediately preceding those, based on descending order of week_end_date.
If the two periods are not exactly 4 weeks each, normalize sales by computing weekly averages before calculating growth.
When calculating metrics by parent account type, merge 'Other' into 'IDN/Hospital' and merge 'Unknown' into 'Community'.
Growth formula: ((current - previous) / previous) * 100; if previous = 0 and current > 0 then growth = 100; if previous = 0 and current = 0 then growth = 0.
All growth metrics must be expressed as percentages and rounded to zero decimal places (integers).
All sales outputs are integers; ensure any percentage point differences are also rounded to integers.
Use overall national sales only for the national benchmark; no additional denominators are applied unless explicitly requested.

2026-02-11T10:47:07.469494+00:00Z.final_sql:
WITH
max_week_cte AS (
  SELECT MAX(week_end_date) AS max_week_end_date
  FROM drug_sales
),
ranked_weeks_cte AS (
  SELECT
    week_end_date,
    week_rank,
    CASE
      WHEN week_rank BETWEEN 1 AND 4 THEN 'current'
      WHEN week_rank BETWEEN 5 AND 8 THEN 'previous'
      ELSE NULL
    END AS period_flag
  FROM (
    SELECT
      w.week_end_date,
      DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY w.week_end_date DESC) AS week_rank
    FROM (SELECT DISTINCT week_end_date FROM drug_sales) w
    CROSS JOIN max_week_cte m
    WHERE w.week_end_date <= m.max_week_end_date
  ) rw
),
selected_weeks AS (
  SELECT week_end_date, period_flag
  FROM ranked_weeks_cte
  WHERE week_rank <= 8
),
week_counts AS (
  SELECT
    SUM(CASE WHEN period_flag = 'current' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS current_week_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN period_flag = 'previous' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS previous_week_count
  FROM selected_weeks
),
base_data_cte AS (
  SELECT
    ts.week_end_date,
    CASE
      WHEN ts.parent_account_type IN ('IDN/Hospital','Other') THEN 'IDN/Hospital'
      WHEN ts.parent_account_type = 'Unknown' THEN 'Community'
      ELSE ts.parent_account_type
    END AS parent_account_type_merged,
    ts.parent_bc_segment,
    sw.period_flag,
    ts.qty_sold_pu
  FROM drug_sales ts
  INNER JOIN selected_weeks sw
    ON ts.week_end_date = sw.week_end_date
),
grouped_sales_by_dim_period AS (
  SELECT
    parent_account_type_merged,
    parent_bc_segment,
    period_flag,
    SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS sales_qty
  FROM base_data_cte
  GROUP BY parent_account_type_merged, parent_bc_segment, period_flag
),
pivot_group_sales AS (
  SELECT
    parent_account_type_merged,
    parent_bc_segment,
    SUM(CASE WHEN period_flag = 'current' THEN sales_qty ELSE 0 END) AS current_4wk_sales,
    SUM(CASE WHEN period_flag = 'previous' THEN sales_qty ELSE 0 END) AS previous_4wk_sales
  FROM grouped_sales_by_dim_period
  GROUP BY parent_account_type_merged, parent_bc_segment
),
group_growth_calc AS (
  SELECT
    pgs.parent_account_type_merged,
    pgs.parent_bc_segment,
    pgs.current_4wk_sales,
    pgs.previous_4wk_sales,
    CAST(ROUND(
      CASE
        WHEN COALESCE(pgs.previous_4wk_sales / NULLIF(wc.previous_week_count, 0), 0) = 0
             AND COALESCE(pgs.current_4wk_sales / NULLIF(wc.current_week_count, 0), 0) > 0 THEN 100
        WHEN COALESCE(pgs.previous_4wk_sales / NULLIF(wc.previous_week_count, 0), 0) = 0
             AND COALESCE(pgs.current_4wk_sales / NULLIF(wc.current_week_count, 0), 0) = 0 THEN 0
        ELSE (
          (
            (pgs.current_4wk_sales / NULLIF(wc.current_week_count, 0)) -
            (pgs.previous_4wk_sales / NULLIF(wc.previous_week_count, 0))
          ) /
          (pgs.previous_4wk_sales / NULLIF(wc.previous_week_count, 0))
        ) * 100
      END, 0) AS SIGNED) AS group_growth_pct
  FROM pivot_group_sales pgs
  CROSS JOIN week_counts wc
),
national_sales_by_period AS (
  SELECT
    SUM(CASE WHEN period_flag = 'current' THEN qty_sold_pu ELSE 0 END) AS national_current_4wk_sales,
    SUM(CASE WHEN period_flag = 'previous' THEN qty_sold_pu ELSE 0 END) AS national_previous_4wk_sales
  FROM base_data_cte
),
national_growth_calc AS (
  SELECT
    nsp.national_current_4wk_sales,
    nsp.national_previous_4wk_sales,
    CAST(ROUND(
      CASE
        WHEN COALESCE(nsp.national_previous_4wk_sales / NULLIF(wc.previous_week_count, 0), 0) = 0
             AND COALESCE(nsp.national_current_4wk_sales / NULLIF(wc.current_week_count, 0), 0) > 0 THEN 100
        WHEN COALESCE(nsp.national_previous_4wk_sales / NULLIF(wc.previous_week_count, 0), 0) = 0
             AND COALESCE(nsp.national_current_4wk_sales / NULLIF(wc.current_week_count, 0), 0) = 0 THEN 0
        ELSE (
          (
            (nsp.national_current_4wk_sales / NULLIF(wc.current_week_count, 0)) -
            (nsp.national_previous_4wk_sales / NULLIF(wc.previous_week_count, 0))
          ) /
          (nsp.national_previous_4wk_sales / NULLIF(wc.previous_week_count, 0))
        ) * 100
      END, 0) AS SIGNED) AS national_growth_pct
  FROM national_sales_by_period nsp
  CROSS JOIN week_counts wc
)
SELECT
  g.parent_account_type_merged,
  g.parent_bc_segment,
  g.current_4wk_sales,
  g.previous_4wk_sales,
  g.group_growth_pct,
  n.national_current_4wk_sales,
  n.national_previous_4wk_sales,
  n.national_growth_pct,
  CAST(ROUND(g.group_growth_pct - n.national_growth_pct, 0) AS SIGNED) AS growth_vs_national_pct_point_diff
FROM group_growth_calc g
CROSS JOIN national_growth_calc n
ORDER BY
  g.parent_account_type_merged ASC,
  g.parent_bc_segment ASC;

2026-02-11T10:51:53.487976+00:00Z.question: what is the sales contribution of BC potential segments within each parent account type

query_decomposition.intent_summary: Compute each BC potential segment's sales contribution within each parent account type over the most recent 52 weeks, after normalizing parent account type categories per rules.

subqueries.name: max_date_cte
subqueries.purpose: Identify the most recent week_end_date in the dataset
subqueries.logic: Compute MAX(week_end_date) from drug_sales

subqueries.name: windowed_sales_cte
subqueries.purpose: Restrict data to the most recent 52 weeks relative to max week_end_date
subqueries.logic: Select rows from drug_sales where week_end_date is between (MAX(week_end_date) - 51 weeks) and MAX(week_end_date)

subqueries.name: normalized_accounts_cte
subqueries.purpose: Normalize parent account type per business rule before aggregation
subqueries.logic: From windowed_sales_cte, create a derived column parent_account_type_normalized as: when parent_account_type = 'Other' then 'IDN/Hospital'; when parent_account_type = 'Unknown' then 'Community'; else parent_account_type

subqueries.name: parent_type_totals_cte
subqueries.purpose: Compute total sales per normalized parent account type for contribution denominators
subqueries.logic: From normalized_accounts_cte, group by parent_account_type_normalized and compute SUM(qty_sold_pu) as parent_type_total_sales_qty

subqueries.name: segment_sales_cte
subqueries.purpose: Compute sales per normalized parent account type and BC segment
subqueries.logic: From normalized_accounts_cte, group by parent_account_type_normalized and parent_bc_segment and compute SUM(qty_sold_pu) as segment_sales_qty

subqueries.name: contribution_calc_cte
subqueries.purpose: Calculate segment contribution within each parent account type
subqueries.logic: Join segment_sales_cte to parent_type_totals_cte on parent_account_type_normalized; compute segment_contribution_pct = ROUND(100 * segment_sales_qty / NULLIF(parent_type_total_sales_qty, 0), 0); coalesce to 0 if denominator is 0

validation_rules: Default to the most recent 52 weeks based on MAX(week_end_date); do not use system date.
Apply the rolling window relative to MAX(week_end_date) from the data.
Normalize parent account types before aggregation: map 'Other' to 'IDN/Hospital' and 'Unknown' to 'Community'.
Compute contribution within each normalized parent account type: denominator is total sales for that normalized parent account type over the same 52-week window.
Handle division by zero by returning 0 for segment_contribution_pct when parent_type_total_sales_qty is 0.
Include all BC segments, including 'Unknown', as distinct categories.
All metric outputs must be rounded to zero decimals and returned as integers.
No time-period comparison is requested; weekly average normalization is not applicable.

2026-02-11T10:51:53.487976+00:00Z.final_sql:
WITH
max_date_cte AS (
  SELECT MAX(week_end_date) AS max_week_end_date
  FROM drug_sales
),
windowed_sales_cte AS (
  SELECT ts.*
  FROM drug_sales ts
  JOIN max_date_cte m ON 1=1
  WHERE ts.week_end_date BETWEEN DATE_SUB(m.max_week_end_date, INTERVAL 51 WEEK) AND m.max_week_end_date
),
normalized_accounts_cte AS (
  SELECT
    CASE
      WHEN parent_account_type = 'Other' THEN 'IDN/Hospital'
      WHEN parent_account_type = 'Unknown' THEN 'Community'
      ELSE parent_account_type
    END AS parent_account_type_normalized,
    parent_bc_segment,
    qty_sold_pu
  FROM windowed_sales_cte
),
parent_type_totals_cte AS (
  SELECT
    parent_account_type_normalized,
    CAST(ROUND(SUM(qty_sold_pu), 0) AS SIGNED) AS parent_type_total_sales_qty
  FROM normalized_accounts_cte
  GROUP BY parent_account_type_normalized
),
segment_sales_cte AS (
  SELECT
    parent_account_type_normalized,
    parent_bc_segment,
    CAST(ROUND(SUM(qty_sold_pu), 0) AS SIGNED) AS segment_sales_qty
  FROM normalized_accounts_cte
  GROUP BY parent_account_type_normalized, parent_bc_segment
),
contribution_calc_cte AS (
  SELECT
    ss.parent_account_type_normalized,
    ss.parent_bc_segment,
    ss.segment_sales_qty,
    CASE
      WHEN pt.parent_type_total_sales_qty IS NULL OR pt.parent_type_total_sales_qty = 0 THEN 0
      ELSE CAST(ROUND(100 * ss.segment_sales_qty / pt.parent_type_total_sales_qty, 0) AS SIGNED)
    END AS segment_contribution_pct
  FROM segment_sales_cte ss
  JOIN parent_type_totals_cte pt
    ON ss.parent_account_type_normalized = pt.parent_account_type_normalized
)
SELECT
  parent_account_type_normalized,
  parent_bc_segment,
  segment_sales_qty,
  segment_contribution_pct
FROM contribution_calc_cte
ORDER BY parent_account_type_normalized ASC, segment_contribution_pct DESC;
