2026-02-17T10:39:36.711748+00:00Z.question: Provide sales contribution by Area and estimate growth in recent 4 weeks compared to previous 4 weeks

query_decomposition.intent_summary: Return area-level sales contribution to national total for the most recent 52 weeks and the percentage growth for recent 4 weeks versus the immediately preceding 4 weeks, display the corresponding sales volumes, and include a single National row in the output.

subqueries.name: max_date_cte
subqueries.purpose: Identify the most recent week_end_date in the dataset
subqueries.logic: Compute MAX(week_end_date) from drug_sales

subqueries.name: r52w_weeks_cte
subqueries.purpose: Define the recent 52-week window based on the latest data
subqueries.logic: From distinct week_end_date values that are <= max_date from max_date_cte, select the 52 most recent distinct dates

subqueries.name: r4w_weeks_cte
subqueries.purpose: Define the recent 4-week window for growth calculation
subqueries.logic: From distinct week_end_date values that are <= max_date from max_date_cte, select the 4 most recent distinct dates

subqueries.name: p4w_weeks_cte
subqueries.purpose: Define the previous 4-week window immediately preceding the recent 4 weeks for growth calculation
subqueries.logic: From distinct week_end_date values that are < MIN(week_end_date) in r4w_weeks_cte, select the next 4 most recent distinct dates

subqueries.name: r52w_area_sales_cte
subqueries.purpose: Compute area-level sales for the recent 52 weeks
subqueries.logic: Filter drug_sales to rows where week_end_date IN r52w_weeks_cte and qty_sold_pu > 0; group by area_nm and compute SUM(qty_sold_pu) as r52w_sales

subqueries.name: national_r52w_total_cte
subqueries.purpose: Compute national sales total for contribution denominator in the recent 52 weeks
subqueries.logic: Filter drug_sales to rows where week_end_date IN r52w_weeks_cte and qty_sold_pu > 0; compute SUM(qty_sold_pu) as national_r52w_sales (single row)

subqueries.name: r4w_area_sales_cte
subqueries.purpose: Compute area-level sales for the recent 4 weeks (for growth)
subqueries.logic: Filter drug_sales to rows where week_end_date IN r4w_weeks_cte and qty_sold_pu > 0; group by area_nm and compute SUM(qty_sold_pu) as r4w_sales

subqueries.name: p4w_area_sales_cte
subqueries.purpose: Compute area-level sales for the previous 4 weeks (for growth)
subqueries.logic: Filter drug_sales to rows where week_end_date IN p4w_weeks_cte and qty_sold_pu > 0; group by area_nm and compute SUM(qty_sold_pu) as p4w_sales

subqueries.name: national_r4w_total_cte
subqueries.purpose: Compute national recent 4-week total sales for the National row growth calculation
subqueries.logic: Filter drug_sales to rows where week_end_date IN r4w_weeks_cte and qty_sold_pu > 0; compute SUM(qty_sold_pu) as national_r4w_sales (single row)

subqueries.name: national_p4w_total_cte
subqueries.purpose: Compute national previous 4-week total sales for the National row growth calculation
subqueries.logic: Filter drug_sales to rows where week_end_date IN p4w_weeks_cte and qty_sold_pu > 0; compute SUM(qty_sold_pu) as national_p4w_sales (single row)

subqueries.name: area_metrics_cte
subqueries.purpose: Combine 52-week contribution and 4-week growth metrics at area level and include volume sales
subqueries.logic: Full outer join r52w_area_sales_cte, r4w_area_sales_cte, and p4w_area_sales_cte on area_nm; replace NULL r52w_sales, r4w_sales, or p4w_sales with 0; cross join national_r52w_total_cte. Compute: contribution_pct = CASE WHEN national_r52w_sales > 0 THEN ROUND((r52w_sales / national_r52w_sales) * 100, 0) ELSE 0 END; growth_pct = CASE WHEN p4w_sales = 0 AND r4w_sales > 0 THEN 100 WHEN p4w_sales = 0 AND r4w_sales = 0 THEN 0 ELSE ROUND(((r4w_sales - p4w_sales) / p4w_sales) * 100, 0) END.

subqueries.name: national_metrics_cte
subqueries.purpose: Create a single National row with the same metrics as areas
subqueries.logic: Cross join national_r52w_total_cte, national_r4w_total_cte, and national_p4w_total_cte; set area_nm = 'National'; set r52w_sales = national_r52w_sales; set r4w_sales = national_r4w_sales; set p4w_sales = national_p4w_sales; compute contribution_pct = CASE WHEN national_r52w_sales > 0 THEN ROUND((national_r52w_sales / national_r52w_sales) * 100, 0) ELSE 0 END (which yields 100); compute growth_pct = CASE WHEN national_p4w_sales = 0 AND national_r4w_sales > 0 THEN 100 WHEN national_p4w_sales = 0 AND national_r4w_sales = 0 THEN 0 ELSE ROUND(((national_r4w_sales - national_p4w_sales) / national_p4w_sales) * 100, 0) END.

subqueries.name: final_with_nation_cte
subqueries.purpose: Union area metrics with the National row for final output
subqueries.logic: SELECT all columns from area_metrics_cte UNION ALL SELECT the corresponding columns from national_metrics_cte

validation_rules: Include a single 'National' row in the output that aggregates across all areas.
Derive MAX(week_end_date) from data; do not use system date.
Define recent 52-week window as the 52 most recent distinct week_end_date values up to MAX(week_end_date).

Define recent 4-week window as the 4 most recent distinct week_end_date values up to MAX(week_end_date); define previous 4-week window as the 4 distinct week_end_date values immediately preceding the recent 4 weeks.
Use week_end_date for all weekly filters and windowing.
Restrict all computations to rows where qty_sold_pu > 0.
Sales contribution denominator is national total sales for the recent 52 weeks across all areas.
Growth is computed on totals since both periods are 4 weeks; if period lengths differ, use weekly averages before comparison.
If previous period value is 0 and current period value is > 0, set growth to 100%; if both are 0, set growth to 0%.
Round all percentage metrics to zero decimals and present as integers; append '%' to contribution and growth outputs.
Include 'Unknown' area as its own group if present; do not drop it.

2026-02-17T10:39:36.711748+00:00Z.final_sql:
WITH
max_date_cte AS (
  SELECT MAX(week_end_date) AS max_week_end_date
  FROM drug_sales
  WHERE qty_sold_pu > 0
),
r52w_weeks_cte AS (
  SELECT week_end_date
  FROM (
    SELECT DISTINCT week_end_date
    FROM drug_sales
    WHERE qty_sold_pu > 0
      AND week_end_date <= (SELECT max_week_end_date FROM max_date_cte)
    ORDER BY week_end_date DESC
    LIMIT 52
  ) d
),
r4w_weeks_cte AS (
  SELECT week_end_date
  FROM (
    SELECT DISTINCT week_end_date
    FROM drug_sales
    WHERE qty_sold_pu > 0
      AND week_end_date <= (SELECT max_week_end_date FROM max_date_cte)
    ORDER BY week_end_date DESC
    LIMIT 4
  ) d
),
p4w_weeks_cte AS (
  SELECT week_end_date
  FROM (
    SELECT DISTINCT week_end_date
    FROM drug_sales
    WHERE qty_sold_pu > 0
      AND week_end_date < (SELECT MIN(week_end_date) FROM r4w_weeks_cte)
    ORDER BY week_end_date DESC
    LIMIT 4
  ) d
),
r52w_area_sales_cte AS (
  SELECT area_nm, SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS r52w_sales
  FROM drug_sales
  WHERE qty_sold_pu > 0
    AND week_end_date IN (SELECT week_end_date FROM r52w_weeks_cte)
  GROUP BY area_nm
),
national_r52w_total_cte AS (
  SELECT SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS national_r52w_sales
  FROM drug_sales
  WHERE qty_sold_pu > 0
    AND week_end_date IN (SELECT week_end_date FROM r52w_weeks_cte)
),
r4w_area_sales_cte AS (
  SELECT area_nm, SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS r4w_sales
  FROM drug_sales
  WHERE qty_sold_pu > 0
    AND week_end_date IN (SELECT week_end_date FROM r4w_weeks_cte)
  GROUP BY area_nm
),
p4w_area_sales_cte AS (
  SELECT area_nm, SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS p4w_sales
  FROM drug_sales
  WHERE qty_sold_pu > 0
    AND week_end_date IN (SELECT week_end_date FROM p4w_weeks_cte)
  GROUP BY area_nm
),
national_r4w_total_cte AS (
  SELECT SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS national_r4w_sales
  FROM drug_sales
  WHERE qty_sold_pu > 0
    AND week_end_date IN (SELECT week_end_date FROM r4w_weeks_cte)
),
national_p4w_total_cte AS (
  SELECT SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS national_p4w_sales
  FROM drug_sales
  WHERE qty_sold_pu > 0
    AND week_end_date IN (SELECT week_end_date FROM p4w_weeks_cte)
),
areas_cte AS (
  SELECT area_nm FROM r52w_area_sales_cte
  UNION
  SELECT area_nm FROM r4w_area_sales_cte
  UNION
  SELECT area_nm FROM p4w_area_sales_cte
),
area_metrics_cte AS (
  SELECT
    a.area_nm,
    COALESCE(r52.r52w_sales, 0) AS r52w_sales,
    COALESCE(r4.r4w_sales, 0) AS r4w_sales,
    COALESCE(p4.p4w_sales, 0) AS p4w_sales,
    n52.national_r52w_sales
  FROM areas_cte a
  LEFT JOIN r52w_area_sales_cte r52 ON r52.area_nm = a.area_nm
  LEFT JOIN r4w_area_sales_cte r4 ON r4.area_nm = a.area_nm
  LEFT JOIN p4w_area_sales_cte p4 ON p4.area_nm = a.area_nm
  CROSS JOIN national_r52w_total_cte n52
),
area_metrics_formatted_cte AS (
  SELECT
    area_nm,
    r52w_sales AS r52w_sales_volume,
    r4w_sales AS r4w_sales_volume,
    p4w_sales AS p4w_sales_volume,
    CONCAT(
      ROUND(
        CASE
          WHEN national_r52w_sales > 0
            THEN (r52w_sales / NULLIF(national_r52w_sales, 0)) * 100
          ELSE 0
        END, 0
      ), '%'
    ) AS contribution_pct_recent_52w,
    CASE
      WHEN p4w_sales = 0 AND r4w_sales > 0 THEN '100%'
      WHEN p4w_sales = 0 AND r4w_sales = 0 THEN '0%'
      ELSE CONCAT(ROUND(((r4w_sales - p4w_sales) / NULLIF(p4w_sales, 0)) * 100, 0), '%')
    END AS growth_pct_recent_4w_vs_previous_4w,
    CASE
      WHEN national_r52w_sales > 0
        THEN ROUND(((r52w_sales / NULLIF(national_r52w_sales, 0)) * 100), 0)
      ELSE 0
    END AS contribution_pct_value
  FROM area_metrics_cte
),
national_metrics_cte AS (
  SELECT
    'National' AS area_nm,
    n52.national_r52w_sales AS r52w_sales_volume,
    n4.national_r4w_sales AS r4w_sales_volume,
    p4.national_p4w_sales AS p4w_sales_volume,
    '100%' AS contribution_pct_recent_52w,
    CASE
      WHEN p4.national_p4w_sales = 0 AND n4.national_r4w_sales > 0 THEN '100%'
      WHEN p4.national_p4w_sales = 0 AND n4.national_r4w_sales = 0 THEN '0%'
      ELSE CONCAT(ROUND(((n4.national_r4w_sales - p4.national_p4w_sales) / NULLIF(p4.national_p4w_sales, 0)) * 100, 0), '%')
    END AS growth_pct_recent_4w_vs_previous_4w,
    100 AS contribution_pct_value
  FROM national_r52w_total_cte n52
  CROSS JOIN national_r4w_total_cte n4
  CROSS JOIN national_p4w_total_cte p4
),
final_with_nation_cte AS (
  SELECT area_nm, r52w_sales_volume, r4w_sales_volume, p4w_sales_volume, contribution_pct_recent_52w, growth_pct_recent_4w_vs_previous_4w, contribution_pct_value
  FROM area_metrics_formatted_cte
  UNION ALL
  SELECT area_nm, r52w_sales_volume, r4w_sales_volume, p4w_sales_volume, contribution_pct_recent_52w, growth_pct_recent_4w_vs_previous_4w, contribution_pct_value
  FROM national_metrics_cte
)
SELECT
  area_nm,
  r52w_sales_volume,
  r4w_sales_volume,
  p4w_sales_volume,
  contribution_pct_recent_52w,
  growth_pct_recent_4w_vs_previous_4w
FROM final_with_nation_cte
ORDER BY contribution_pct_value DESC;

2026-02-17T11:01:55.371837+00:00Z.question: Estimate the sales growth by Area and Parent Account Type in recent 4 weeks compared to previous 4 weeks?

query_decomposition.intent_summary: Compute sales growth percentage by Area and Parent Account Type comparing Recent 4 Weeks (R4W) versus Previous 4 Weeks (P4W), anchored to the most recent week_end_date, merging parent account types per rules.

subqueries.name: max_date_cte
subqueries.purpose: Identify the most recent week_end_date to anchor the 4-week windows.
subqueries.logic: Select MAX(week_end_date) AS max_week_end_date from drug_sales.

subqueries.name: weeks_cte
subqueries.purpose: Define the last 8 distinct calendar weeks and label them as R4W (ranks 1-4) and P4W (ranks 5-8).
subqueries.logic: From drug_sales, select DISTINCT week_end_date where week_end_date <= (SELECT max_week_end_date FROM max_date_cte). Assign DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY week_end_date DESC) AS week_rank. Keep rows with week_rank BETWEEN 1 AND 8. Create period_flag: 'R4W' when week_rank BETWEEN 1 AND 4, 'P4W' when week_rank BETWEEN 5 AND 8.

subqueries.name: period_weeks_count_cte
subqueries.purpose: Count the number of distinct weeks in each period window to enable normalization if periods differ in length.
subqueries.logic: From weeks_cte, compute COUNT(DISTINCT week_end_date) per period_flag to produce two rows: one for 'R4W' and one for 'P4W'.

subqueries.name: base_sales_cte
subqueries.purpose: Aggregate sales by Area and merged Parent Account Type within each period window using only positive sales, and also produce a national rollup.
subqueries.logic: Join drug_sales t to weeks_cte w on t.week_end_date = w.week_end_date. Filter t.qty_sold_pu > 0. Derive parent_account_type_merged as: when t.parent_account_type = 'Other' then 'IDN/Hospital'; when t.parent_account_type = 'Unknown' then 'Community'; else t.parent_account_type. Aggregate using GROUPING SETS to produce both area-level and national totals: GROUPING SETS ((t.area_nm, parent_account_type_merged, w.period_flag), (parent_account_type_merged, w.period_flag)). Compute SUM(t.qty_sold_pu) AS period_sales. For the rollup rows where area_nm is NULL, they represent national totals.

subqueries.name: final_cte
subqueries.purpose: Pivot period sales, normalize if needed, compute growth percentage per group, and label national rows.
subqueries.logic: From base_sales_cte, pivot/conditional-aggregate to get, per (area_nm, parent_account_type_merged), recent_4w_sales = SUM(CASE WHEN period_flag = 'R4W' THEN period_sales ELSE 0 END) and previous_4w_sales = SUM(CASE WHEN period_flag = 'P4W' THEN period_sales ELSE 0 END). Cross join period_weeks_count_cte twice (once filtered to 'R4W' and once to 'P4W') to obtain r_weeks_count and p_weeks_count. Compute normalized_recent = CASE WHEN r_weeks_count > 0 THEN recent_4w_sales * 1.0 / r_weeks_count ELSE 0 END and normalized_prior = CASE WHEN p_weeks_count > 0 THEN previous_4w_sales * 1.0 / p_weeks_count ELSE 0 END. Compute sales_growth_percent with rules: if previous_4w_sales = 0 AND recent_4w_sales > 0 THEN 100; else if normalized_prior = 0 AND normalized_recent = 0 THEN 0; else ROUND(((normalized_recent - normalized_prior) / NULLIF(normalized_prior, 0)) * 100, 0). Create display_area_nm = CASE WHEN area_nm IS NULL THEN 'Nation' ELSE area_nm END. Output display_area_nm AS area_nm, parent_account_type_merged, recent_4w_sales, previous_4w_sales, and sales_growth_percent suffixed with '%'.

validation_rules: Use MAX(week_end_date) from the dataset to define 'recent'; do not use the system date.
Windows must be determined using week_end_date only; months/quarters should not be used.
Exactly 8 most recent distinct weeks: ranks 1-4 = R4W and 5-8 = P4W.
Restrict all sales computations to rows where qty_sold_pu > 0.
Before aggregation, merge Parent Account Types: map 'Other' to 'IDN/Hospital' and 'Unknown' to 'Community'.
Group results by area_nm and the merged parent account type, and also include a national rollup across all areas. Represent national rows with area_nm = 'Nation'.
If R4W and P4W have different week counts (due to data sparsity), compute growth using average weekly sales (total/period_weeks) for each period.
Growth rule: if previous period sales are 0 and current period sales are > 0, set growth to '100%'. If both periods are 0, set growth to '0%'. Otherwise compute ((current - prior) / prior) * 100.
All growth outputs must be rounded to 0 decimals, returned as integers, and suffixed with '%'.
Include 'Unknown' areas if present; do not filter them out.
CTE naming must be case-consistent: all references use 'max_date_cte' exactly as defined (no case mismatches).

2026-02-17T11:01:55.371837+00:00Z.final_sql:
WITH
max_date_cte AS (
  SELECT MAX(week_end_date) AS max_week_end_date
  FROM drug_sales
),
weeks_raw AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT t.week_end_date
  FROM drug_sales t
  WHERE t.week_end_date <= (SELECT max_week_end_date FROM max_date_cte)
),
weeks_ranked AS (
  SELECT
    wr.week_end_date,
    DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY wr.week_end_date DESC) AS week_rank
  FROM weeks_raw wr
),
weeks_cte AS (
  SELECT
    week_end_date,
    CASE
      WHEN week_rank BETWEEN 1 AND 4 THEN 'R4W'
      WHEN week_rank BETWEEN 5 AND 8 THEN 'P4W'
    END AS period_flag
  FROM weeks_ranked
  WHERE week_rank BETWEEN 1 AND 8
),
period_weeks_count_cte AS (
  SELECT
    period_flag,
    COUNT(DISTINCT week_end_date) AS weeks_count
  FROM weeks_cte
  GROUP BY period_flag
),
r_weeks_count_cte AS (
  SELECT COALESCE(MAX(weeks_count), 0) AS r_weeks_count
  FROM period_weeks_count_cte
  WHERE period_flag = 'R4W'
),
p_weeks_count_cte AS (
  SELECT COALESCE(MAX(weeks_count), 0) AS p_weeks_count
  FROM period_weeks_count_cte
  WHERE period_flag = 'P4W'
),
base_sales_area AS (
  SELECT
    t.area_nm,
    CASE
      WHEN t.parent_account_type = 'Other' THEN 'IDN/Hospital'
      WHEN t.parent_account_type = 'Unknown' THEN 'Community'
      ELSE t.parent_account_type
    END AS parent_account_type_merged,
    w.period_flag,
    SUM(t.qty_sold_pu) AS period_sales
  FROM drug_sales t
  JOIN weeks_cte w
    ON t.week_end_date = w.week_end_date
  WHERE t.qty_sold_pu > 0
  GROUP BY
    t.area_nm,
    CASE
      WHEN t.parent_account_type = 'Other' THEN 'IDN/Hospital'
      WHEN t.parent_account_type = 'Unknown' THEN 'Community'
      ELSE t.parent_account_type
    END,
    w.period_flag
),
base_sales_nation AS (
  SELECT
    NULL AS area_nm,
    CASE
      WHEN t.parent_account_type = 'Other' THEN 'IDN/Hospital'
      WHEN t.parent_account_type = 'Unknown' THEN 'Community'
      ELSE t.parent_account_type
    END AS parent_account_type_merged,
    w.period_flag,
    SUM(t.qty_sold_pu) AS period_sales
  FROM drug_sales t
  JOIN weeks_cte w
    ON t.week_end_date = w.week_end_date
  WHERE t.qty_sold_pu > 0
  GROUP BY
    CASE
      WHEN t.parent_account_type = 'Other' THEN 'IDN/Hospital'
      WHEN t.parent_account_type = 'Unknown' THEN 'Community'
      ELSE t.parent_account_type
    END,
    w.period_flag
),
base_sales_cte AS (
  SELECT * FROM base_sales_area
  UNION ALL
  SELECT * FROM base_sales_nation
),
pivot_cte AS (
  SELECT
    b.area_nm,
    b.parent_account_type_merged,
    SUM(CASE WHEN b.period_flag = 'R4W' THEN b.period_sales ELSE 0 END) AS recent_4w_sales,
    SUM(CASE WHEN b.period_flag = 'P4W' THEN b.period_sales ELSE 0 END) AS previous_4w_sales
  FROM base_sales_cte b
  GROUP BY b.area_nm, b.parent_account_type_merged
),
final_cte AS (
  SELECT
    CASE WHEN p.area_nm IS NULL THEN 'Nation' ELSE p.area_nm END AS area_nm,
    p.parent_account_type_merged,
    p.recent_4w_sales,
    p.previous_4w_sales,
    CASE
      WHEN p.previous_4w_sales = 0 AND p.recent_4w_sales > 0 THEN CONCAT('100', '%')
      WHEN COALESCE(p.recent_4w_sales * 1.0 / NULLIF(rwc.r_weeks_count, 0), 0) = 0
           AND COALESCE(p.previous_4w_sales * 1.0 / NULLIF(pwc.p_weeks_count, 0), 0) = 0
        THEN CONCAT('0', '%')
      ELSE CONCAT(
             ROUND(
               (
                 (p.recent_4w_sales * 1.0 / NULLIF(rwc.r_weeks_count, 0)) -
                 (p.previous_4w_sales * 1.0 / NULLIF(pwc.p_weeks_count, 0))
               ) / NULLIF((p.previous_4w_sales * 1.0 / NULLIF(pwc.p_weeks_count, 0)), 0) * 100
               , 0
             ),
             '%'
           )
    END AS sales_growth_percent
  FROM pivot_cte p
  CROSS JOIN r_weeks_count_cte rwc
  CROSS JOIN p_weeks_count_cte pwc
)
SELECT
  area_nm,
  parent_account_type_merged,
  recent_4w_sales,
  previous_4w_sales,
  sales_growth_percent
FROM final_cte
ORDER BY area_nm ASC, parent_account_type_merged ASC;

2026-02-18T05:49:19.539644+00:00Z.question: what is the sales growth in recent 4 weeks compared to previous 13 weeks by child account type and how does that compare with nation

query_decomposition.intent_summary: Compute sales growth for recent 4 weeks vs prior 13 weeks by child account type, normalized to weekly averages, and compare each segment to national performance. Include absolute sales for both periods and national benchmarks.

subqueries.name: max_date_cte
subqueries.purpose: Identify the most recent week_end_date based on available data and qty_sold_pu > 0
subqueries.logic: Compute MAX(week_end_date) from drug_sales where qty_sold_pu > 0

subqueries.name: distinct_weeks_cte
subqueries.purpose: Enumerate distinct weeks up to the max date for precise rolling window selection
subqueries.logic: Select DISTINCT week_end_date where qty_sold_pu > 0 and week_end_date <= (select max_week from max_date_cte).

subqueries.name: recent_4w_weeks_cte
subqueries.purpose: Determine the most recent 4 distinct weeks relative to max week_end_date
subqueries.logic: From distinct_weeks_cte, order by week_end_date DESC and keep the top 4 rows as recent 4 weeks.

subqueries.name: prior_13w_weeks_cte
subqueries.purpose: Determine the 13 distinct weeks immediately preceding the recent 4-week window (non-overlapping)
subqueries.logic: Find the minimum week_end_date from recent_4w_weeks_cte as boundary_week. From distinct_weeks_cte, select rows with week_end_date < boundary_week, order by week_end_date DESC, and keep the top 13 rows.

subqueries.name: child_type_mapping_cte
subqueries.purpose: Apply account type merge rules at child level
subqueries.logic: Derive child_account_type_merged as: CASE WHEN child_account_type = 'Unknown' THEN 'Community' WHEN child_account_type IN ('Other','Others') THEN 'IDN/Hospital' ELSE child_account_type END, alongside all original columns.

subqueries.name: r4w_child_sales_cte
subqueries.purpose: Aggregate recent 4-week sales by merged child account type
subqueries.logic: Join child_type_mapping_cte to recent_4w_weeks_cte on week_end_date and SUM(qty_sold_pu) grouped by child_account_type_merged. Also carry a constant week_count_r4w = 4 for normalization.

subqueries.name: p13w_child_sales_cte
subqueries.purpose: Aggregate prior 13-week sales by merged child account type
subqueries.logic: Join child_type_mapping_cte to prior_13w_weeks_cte on week_end_date and SUM(qty_sold_pu) grouped by child_account_type_merged. Also carry a constant week_count_p13w = 13 for normalization.

subqueries.name: child_combined_cte
subqueries.purpose: Combine R4W and P13W totals per child account type and compute weekly averages
subqueries.logic: FULL OUTER JOIN r4w_child_sales_cte and p13w_child_sales_cte on child_account_type_merged. Compute r4w_total_sales, p13w_total_sales. Compute r4w_avg_weekly = ROUND(r4w_total_sales / 4, 0) and p13w_avg_weekly = ROUND(p13w_total_sales / 13, 0).

subqueries.name: r4w_national_sales_cte
subqueries.purpose: Aggregate recent 4-week national sales
subqueries.logic: Join drug_sales (filtered to qty_sold_pu > 0) to recent_4w_weeks_cte on week_end_date and SUM(qty_sold_pu) across all rows (no group). Also carry week_count_r4w_nation = 4.

subqueries.name: p13w_national_sales_cte
subqueries.purpose: Aggregate prior 13-week national sales
subqueries.logic: Join drug_sales (filtered to qty_sold_pu > 0) to prior_13w_weeks_cte on week_end_date and SUM(qty_sold_pu) across all rows (no group). Also carry week_count_p13w_nation = 13.

subqueries.name: national_combined_cte
subqueries.purpose: Compute national weekly averages for both windows
subqueries.logic: Join r4w_national_sales_cte and p13w_national_sales_cte (cartesian as both are single-row). Compute national_r4w_avg_weekly = ROUND(r4w_total_sales_nation / 4, 0) and national_p13w_avg_weekly = ROUND(p13w_total_sales_nation / 13, 0).

subqueries.name: final_result_cte
subqueries.purpose: Produce per child account type results with national benchmarks and growth metrics
subqueries.logic: CROSS JOIN child_combined_cte with national_combined_cte to attach national metrics to each child_account_type_merged row. Compute growth_child_pct as: if p13w_avg_weekly = 0 and r4w_avg_weekly > 0 then '100%' else ROUND(((r4w_avg_weekly - p13w_avg_weekly) / NULLIF(p13w_avg_weekly,0)) * 100, 0) || '%'. Compute growth_nation_pct similarly using national averages with the same 100% rule. Compute abs_change_in_avg_weekly_sales = ROUND(r4w_avg_weekly - p13w_avg_weekly, 0). Compute growth_gap_vs_nation_pp = ROUND((child_growth_pct_value - nation_growth_pct_value), 0) || '%', where child_growth_pct_value and nation_growth_pct_value are the integer percentage numbers before appending '%'. Ensure all growth fields have '%' appended.

validation_rules: Use MAX(week_end_date) from data; do not use system date.
Recent 4-week window and prior 13-week window must be computed from distinct week_end_date values relative to MAX(week_end_date).
Prior 13-week window must not overlap with recent 4-week window.
Normalize comparisons using weekly averages: divide R4W totals by 4 and P13W totals by 13 before computing growth.
If previous period (P13W average) is 0 and current period (R4W average) > 0, set growth to 100%. If both are 0, set growth to 0%.
All growth and percentage outputs must be rounded to zero decimals and suffixed with '%'.
All absolute sales and average values must be rounded to zero decimals (integers).
Always include absolute sales totals for both R4W and P13W alongside growth metrics.
Merge child_account_type 'Unknown' into 'Community'. If any 'Other'/'Others' appears, merge into 'IDN/Hospital'.
Filter all computations to rows where qty_sold_pu > 0.
Use week_end_date for all weekly window logic.
Compute national metrics over the same windows and filters and attach them to each child account type row for comparison.
Do not aggregate to parent entities; use only child account type segmentation as requested.

2026-02-18T05:49:19.539644+00:00Z.final_sql:
WITH
max_date_cte AS (
  SELECT MAX(week_end_date) AS max_week
  FROM drug_sales
  WHERE qty_sold_pu > 0
),
distinct_weeks_cte AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT t.week_end_date
  FROM drug_sales t
  JOIN max_date_cte m ON 1=1
  WHERE t.qty_sold_pu > 0
    AND t.week_end_date <= m.max_week
),
recent_4w_weeks_cte AS (
  SELECT week_end_date
  FROM (
    SELECT week_end_date,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY week_end_date DESC) AS rn
    FROM distinct_weeks_cte
  ) x
  WHERE rn <= 4
),
prior_13w_weeks_cte AS (
  SELECT week_end_date
  FROM (
    SELECT d.week_end_date,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY d.week_end_date DESC) AS rn
    FROM distinct_weeks_cte d
    JOIN (
      SELECT MIN(week_end_date) AS boundary_week FROM recent_4w_weeks_cte
    ) b ON d.week_end_date < b.boundary_week
  ) y
  WHERE rn <= 13
),
child_type_mapping_cte AS (
  SELECT
    t.*,
    CASE
      WHEN t.child_account_type = 'Unknown' THEN 'Community'
      WHEN t.child_account_type IN ('Other','Others') THEN 'IDN/Hospital'
      ELSE t.child_account_type
    END AS child_account_type_merged
  FROM drug_sales t
  WHERE t.qty_sold_pu > 0
),
r4w_child_sales_cte AS (
  SELECT
    m.child_account_type_merged,
    ROUND(SUM(m.qty_sold_pu), 0) AS r4w_total_sales,
    4 AS week_count_r4w
  FROM child_type_mapping_cte m
  JOIN recent_4w_weeks_cte w
    ON m.week_end_date = w.week_end_date
  GROUP BY m.child_account_type_merged
),
p13w_child_sales_cte AS (
  SELECT
    m.child_account_type_merged,
    ROUND(SUM(m.qty_sold_pu), 0) AS p13w_total_sales,
    13 AS week_count_p13w
  FROM child_type_mapping_cte m
  JOIN prior_13w_weeks_cte w
    ON m.week_end_date = w.week_end_date
  GROUP BY m.child_account_type_merged
),
child_types AS (
  SELECT child_account_type_merged FROM r4w_child_sales_cte
  UNION
  SELECT child_account_type_merged FROM p13w_child_sales_cte
),
child_combined_cte AS (
  SELECT
    ct.child_account_type_merged,
    COALESCE(r.r4w_total_sales, 0) AS r4w_total_sales,
    COALESCE(p.p13w_total_sales, 0) AS p13w_total_sales,
    ROUND(COALESCE(r.r4w_total_sales, 0) / 4, 0) AS r4w_avg_weekly,
    ROUND(COALESCE(p.p13w_total_sales, 0) / 13, 0) AS p13w_avg_weekly
  FROM child_types ct
  LEFT JOIN r4w_child_sales_cte r USING (child_account_type_merged)
  LEFT JOIN p13w_child_sales_cte p USING (child_account_type_merged)
),
r4w_national_sales_cte AS (
  SELECT
    ROUND(SUM(t.qty_sold_pu), 0) AS national_r4w_total_sales,
    4 AS week_count_r4w_nation
  FROM drug_sales t
  JOIN recent_4w_weeks_cte w ON t.week_end_date = w.week_end_date
  WHERE t.qty_sold_pu > 0
),
p13w_national_sales_cte AS (
  SELECT
    ROUND(SUM(t.qty_sold_pu), 0) AS national_p13w_total_sales,
    13 AS week_count_p13w_nation
  FROM drug_sales t
  JOIN prior_13w_weeks_cte w ON t.week_end_date = w.week_end_date
  WHERE t.qty_sold_pu > 0
),
national_combined_cte AS (
  SELECT
    n4.national_r4w_total_sales,
    n13.national_p13w_total_sales,
    ROUND(n4.national_r4w_total_sales / 4, 0) AS national_r4w_avg_weekly,
    ROUND(n13.national_p13w_total_sales / 13, 0) AS national_p13w_avg_weekly
  FROM r4w_national_sales_cte n4
  CROSS JOIN p13w_national_sales_cte n13
),
final_result_cte AS (
  SELECT
    c.child_account_type_merged,
    ROUND(c.r4w_total_sales, 0) AS r4w_total_sales,
    ROUND(c.p13w_total_sales, 0) AS p13w_total_sales,
    ROUND(c.r4w_avg_weekly, 0) AS r4w_avg_weekly,
    ROUND(c.p13w_avg_weekly, 0) AS p13w_avg_weekly,
    CASE
      WHEN c.p13w_avg_weekly = 0 AND c.r4w_avg_weekly > 0 THEN 100
      WHEN c.p13w_avg_weekly = 0 AND c.r4w_avg_weekly = 0 THEN 0
      ELSE ROUND(((c.r4w_avg_weekly - c.p13w_avg_weekly) / NULLIF(c.p13w_avg_weekly, 0)) * 100, 0)
    END AS growth_child_pct_value,
    ROUND(c.r4w_avg_weekly - c.p13w_avg_weekly, 0) AS abs_change_in_avg_weekly_sales,
    n.national_r4w_total_sales,
    n.national_p13w_total_sales,
    n.national_r4w_avg_weekly,
    n.national_p13w_avg_weekly,
    CASE
      WHEN n.national_p13w_avg_weekly = 0 AND n.national_r4w_avg_weekly > 0 THEN 100
      WHEN n.national_p13w_avg_weekly = 0 AND n.national_r4w_avg_weekly = 0 THEN 0
      ELSE ROUND(((n.national_r4w_avg_weekly - n.national_p13w_avg_weekly) / NULLIF(n.national_p13w_avg_weekly, 0)) * 100, 0)
    END AS growth_nation_pct_value
  FROM child_combined_cte c
  CROSS JOIN national_combined_cte n
)
SELECT
  child_account_type_merged,
  r4w_total_sales,
  p13w_total_sales,
  r4w_avg_weekly,
  p13w_avg_weekly,
  CONCAT(growth_child_pct_value, '%') AS growth_child_pct,
  abs_change_in_avg_weekly_sales,
  national_r4w_total_sales,
  national_p13w_total_sales,
  national_r4w_avg_weekly,
  national_p13w_avg_weekly,
  CONCAT(growth_nation_pct_value, '%') AS growth_nation_pct,
  CONCAT(ROUND(growth_child_pct_value - growth_nation_pct_value, 0), '%') AS growth_gap_vs_nation_pp
FROM final_result_cte
ORDER BY child_account_type_merged;

2026-02-18T06:05:59.942669+00:00Z.question: what is the sales contribution of child accounts by BC potential segment?

query_decomposition.intent_summary: Compute sales contribution of child accounts by child BC potential segment over the most recent 52 weeks, merging 'Unknown' into 'Low', using national sales as the denominator, and return both absolute sales and percentage contribution.

subqueries.name: max_date_cte
subqueries.purpose: Identify the most recent week_end_date in the dataset
subqueries.logic: Compute MAX(week_end_date) from drug_sales

subqueries.name: r52w_window_cte
subqueries.purpose: Define the rolling 52-week window relative to max week_end_date
subqueries.logic: Using max_date from max_date_cte, derive start_date = max_date - 51 weeks; valid window is week_end_date between start_date and max_date (inclusive)

subqueries.name: base_filtered_cte
subqueries.purpose: Filter to the rolling 52-week window and positive sales only
subqueries.logic: Select rows from drug_sales where week_end_date is within r52w_window_cte and qty_sold_pu > 0

subqueries.name: segment_sales_cte
subqueries.purpose: Compute sales by child BC potential segment with 'Unknown' merged into 'Low'
subqueries.logic: From base_filtered_cte, derive child_bc_segment_merged using CASE: WHEN child_bc_segment IN ('Low','Unknown') THEN 'Low' ELSE child_bc_segment END; group by child_bc_segment_merged and compute SUM(qty_sold_pu) as segment_sales

subqueries.name: national_total_cte
subqueries.purpose: Compute national total sales for the same period to serve as denominator
subqueries.logic: From base_filtered_cte, compute SUM(qty_sold_pu) as national_total_sales

subqueries.name: final_cte
subqueries.purpose: Combine segment sales with national total and compute contribution percentage
subqueries.logic: Cross join segment_sales_cte with national_total_cte and compute contribution_pct = ROUND((segment_sales / NULLIF(national_total_sales, 0)) * 100, 0); if national_total_sales is 0 then contribution_pct = 0; present child_bc_segment_merged as child_bc_segment; ensure contribution_pct has '%' appended; ensure segment_sales are integers

validation_rules: Default time window is the most recent 52 weeks based on MAX(week_end_date); do not use system date
Apply time filter using week_end_date between start_date and max_date (inclusive)
Restrict all computations to rows where qty_sold_pu > 0
Use child_bc_segment for segmentation at child level; do not roll up to parent-level segments
Merge 'Unknown' into 'Low' for child BC potential segment before aggregation and contribution computation
Denominator for contribution is national_total_sales over the same 52-week window
Round contribution percentage to 0 decimals and append '%'; segment_sales must be integers
Always include the absolute segment_sales alongside contribution_pct
If national_total_sales equals 0, set contribution_pct to 0% to avoid division by zero

2026-02-18T06:05:59.942669+00:00Z.final_sql:
WITH
max_date_cte AS (
  SELECT MAX(week_end_date) AS max_date
  FROM drug_sales
),
r52w_window_cte AS (
  SELECT DATE_SUB(max_date, INTERVAL 51 WEEK) AS start_date, max_date
  FROM max_date_cte
),
base_filtered_cte AS (
  SELECT s.*
  FROM drug_sales s
  JOIN r52w_window_cte w
    ON s.week_end_date BETWEEN w.start_date AND w.max_date
  WHERE s.qty_sold_pu > 0
),
segment_sales_cte AS (
  SELECT
    CASE
      WHEN child_bc_segment IN ('Low','Unknown') THEN 'Low'
      ELSE child_bc_segment
    END AS child_bc_segment_merged,
    SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS segment_sales
  FROM base_filtered_cte
  GROUP BY child_bc_segment_merged
),
national_total_cte AS (
  SELECT SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS national_total_sales
  FROM base_filtered_cte
),
final_cte AS (
  SELECT
    s.child_bc_segment_merged AS child_bc_segment,
    CAST(ROUND(s.segment_sales, 0) AS SIGNED) AS segment_sales,
    CASE
      WHEN COALESCE(nt.national_total_sales, 0) = 0 THEN '0%'
      ELSE CONCAT(ROUND((s.segment_sales / NULLIF(nt.national_total_sales, 0)) * 100, 0), '%')
    END AS contribution_pct,
    CASE
      WHEN COALESCE(nt.national_total_sales, 0) = 0 THEN 0
      ELSE ROUND((s.segment_sales / NULLIF(nt.national_total_sales, 0)) * 100, 0)
    END AS contribution_pct_value
  FROM segment_sales_cte s
  CROSS JOIN national_total_cte nt
)
SELECT child_bc_segment, segment_sales, contribution_pct
FROM final_cte
ORDER BY contribution_pct_value DESC, segment_sales DESC;

2026-02-18T06:16:08.301616+00:00Z.question: what is the sales growth in recent 4 weeks by child BC potential segment and how does that compare with nation

query_decomposition.intent_summary: Compute sales growth in the most recent 4 weeks (R4W) by child BC potential segment (with Unknown/Others merged into Low) and compare each segment's growth with national (overall) growth. Include absolute sales volumes for both R4W and the prior 4 weeks (P4W).

subqueries.name: max_week_cte
subqueries.purpose: Identify the most recent week_end_date in the dataset
subqueries.logic: Compute MAX(week_end_date) from drug_sales. Do not use system date.

subqueries.name: weeks_ranked_cte
subqueries.purpose: Rank distinct weeks relative to the most recent week for rolling window selection
subqueries.logic: Select distinct week_end_date and apply ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY week_end_date DESC) AS week_rank.

subqueries.name: r4w_weeks_cte
subqueries.purpose: Define the recent 4-week window
subqueries.logic: Select week_end_date from weeks_ranked_cte where week_rank BETWEEN 1 AND 4.

subqueries.name: p4w_weeks_cte
subqueries.purpose: Define the prior 4-week window immediately before R4W
subqueries.logic: Select week_end_date from weeks_ranked_cte where week_rank BETWEEN 5 AND 8.

subqueries.name: mapped_segments_cte
subqueries.purpose: Normalize child BC potential segments per rules
subqueries.logic: Derive mapped_child_bc_segment as: CASE WHEN child_bc_segment IN ('High','Medium','Low') THEN child_bc_segment ELSE 'Low' END. This merges 'Unknown' and any 'Others' into 'Low' for child_bc_segment metrics.

subqueries.name: segment_r4w_sales_cte
subqueries.purpose: Aggregate R4W sales by mapped child BC segment
subqueries.logic: Join drug_sales to r4w_weeks_cte on week_end_date; filter qty_sold_pu > 0; compute mapped_child_bc_segment via mapped_segments_cte logic; GROUP BY mapped_child_bc_segment; SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS r4w_sales_units.

subqueries.name: segment_p4w_sales_cte
subqueries.purpose: Aggregate P4W sales by mapped child BC segment
subqueries.logic: Join drug_sales to p4w_weeks_cte on week_end_date; filter qty_sold_pu > 0; compute mapped_child_bc_segment via mapped_segments_cte logic; GROUP BY mapped_child_bc_segment; SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS p4w_sales_units.

subqueries.name: nation_r4w_sales_cte
subqueries.purpose: Compute national (overall) R4W sales
subqueries.logic: Join drug_sales to r4w_weeks_cte on week_end_date; filter qty_sold_pu > 0; SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS nation_r4w_sales_units.

subqueries.name: nation_p4w_sales_cte
subqueries.purpose: Compute national (overall) P4W sales
subqueries.logic: Join drug_sales to p4w_weeks_cte on week_end_date; filter qty_sold_pu > 0; SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS nation_p4w_sales_units.

subqueries.name: segment_join_cte
subqueries.purpose: Combine segment-level R4W and P4W volumes
subqueries.logic: FULL OUTER JOIN segment_r4w_sales_cte and segment_p4w_sales_cte on mapped_child_bc_segment; fill missing volumes with 0 to allow growth computation.

subqueries.name: nation_join_cte
subqueries.purpose: Prepare national-level R4W and P4W volumes for comparison
subqueries.logic: CROSS JOIN nation_r4w_sales_cte and nation_p4w_sales_cte to provide national volumes to all rows.

subqueries.name: final_metrics_cte
subqueries.purpose: Compute growth percentages and prepare final per-segment output with national comparison
subqueries.logic: For each mapped_child_bc_segment, compute r4w_growth_pct = CASE WHEN p4w_sales_units = 0 AND r4w_sales_units > 0 THEN 100 WHEN p4w_sales_units = 0 AND r4w_sales_units = 0 THEN 0 ELSE ROUND(((r4w_sales_units - p4w_sales_units) * 100.0) / p4w_sales_units, 0) END. Compute nation_r4w_growth_pct similarly from nation_r4w_sales_units and nation_p4w_sales_units. Optionally compute growth_vs_nation_delta_pct = r4w_growth_pct - nation_r4w_growth_pct. Ensure all percentage outputs are rounded to 0 decimals; append '%' in the final presentation layer.

validation_rules: Use week_end_date for all weekly calculations.
Derive R4W and P4W relative to MAX(week_end_date) in the data; do not use the system date.
R4W and P4W must each contain exactly 4 distinct weeks (week_rank 1-4 and 5-8).
If R4W and P4W are not equal in length, normalize to average weekly sales before computing growth.
Restrict all computations to rows where qty_sold_pu > 0.
Merge 'Unknown' and 'Others' into 'Low' for child_bc_segment before aggregations.
Whenever 'nation' is referenced, include national-level metrics computed over all data matching other filters.
If previous period (P4W) value is 0 and current period (R4W) value is > 0, report growth as 100%. If both are 0, report 0%.
All growth metrics must be expressed in percentage format and rounded to zero decimal places with a '%' suffix.
Always accompany growth percentages with absolute R4W and P4W volume values in the output.

2026-02-18T06:16:08.301616+00:00Z.final_sql:
WITH
max_week_cte AS (
  SELECT MAX(week_end_date) AS max_week_end_date
  FROM drug_sales
),
weeks_ranked_cte AS (
  SELECT
    w.week_end_date,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY w.week_end_date DESC) AS week_rank
  FROM (
    SELECT DISTINCT week_end_date
    FROM drug_sales
    WHERE week_end_date IS NOT NULL
  ) w
),
r4w_weeks_cte AS (
  SELECT week_end_date
  FROM weeks_ranked_cte
  WHERE week_rank BETWEEN 1 AND 4
),
p4w_weeks_cte AS (
  SELECT week_end_date
  FROM weeks_ranked_cte
  WHERE week_rank BETWEEN 5 AND 8
),
r4w_week_count_cte AS (
  SELECT COUNT(*) AS r4w_week_count FROM r4w_weeks_cte
),
p4w_week_count_cte AS (
  SELECT COUNT(*) AS p4w_week_count FROM p4w_weeks_cte
),
segment_r4w_sales_cte AS (
  SELECT
    CASE WHEN child_bc_segment IN ('High','Medium','Low') THEN child_bc_segment ELSE 'Low' END AS mapped_child_bc_segment,
    SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS r4w_sales_units
  FROM drug_sales s
  INNER JOIN r4w_weeks_cte r4w ON s.week_end_date = r4w.week_end_date
  WHERE s.qty_sold_pu > 0
  GROUP BY mapped_child_bc_segment
),
segment_p4w_sales_cte AS (
  SELECT
    CASE WHEN child_bc_segment IN ('High','Medium','Low') THEN child_bc_segment ELSE 'Low' END AS mapped_child_bc_segment,
    SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS p4w_sales_units
  FROM drug_sales s
  INNER JOIN p4w_weeks_cte p4w ON s.week_end_date = p4w.week_end_date
  WHERE s.qty_sold_pu > 0
  GROUP BY mapped_child_bc_segment
),
segment_join_cte AS (
  SELECT
    r.mapped_child_bc_segment,
    r.r4w_sales_units,
    COALESCE(p.p4w_sales_units, 0) AS p4w_sales_units
  FROM segment_r4w_sales_cte r
  LEFT JOIN segment_p4w_sales_cte p
    ON p.mapped_child_bc_segment = r.mapped_child_bc_segment
  UNION ALL
  SELECT
    p.mapped_child_bc_segment,
    0 AS r4w_sales_units,
    p.p4w_sales_units
  FROM segment_p4w_sales_cte p
  LEFT JOIN segment_r4w_sales_cte r
    ON r.mapped_child_bc_segment = p.mapped_child_bc_segment
  WHERE r.mapped_child_bc_segment IS NULL
),
nation_r4w_sales_cte AS (
  SELECT
    SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS nation_r4w_sales_units
  FROM drug_sales s
  INNER JOIN r4w_weeks_cte r4w ON s.week_end_date = r4w.week_end_date
  WHERE s.qty_sold_pu > 0
),
nation_p4w_sales_cte AS (
  SELECT
    SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS nation_p4w_sales_units
  FROM drug_sales s
  INNER JOIN p4w_weeks_cte p4w ON s.week_end_date = p4w.week_end_date
  WHERE s.qty_sold_pu > 0
),
nation_join_cte AS (
  SELECT
    CAST(ROUND(nr.nation_r4w_sales_units, 0) AS SIGNED) AS nation_r4w_sales_units,
    CAST(ROUND(np.nation_p4w_sales_units, 0) AS SIGNED) AS nation_p4w_sales_units
  FROM nation_r4w_sales_cte nr
  CROSS JOIN nation_p4w_sales_cte np
),
final_metrics_cte AS (
  SELECT
    sj.mapped_child_bc_segment,
    CAST(ROUND(sj.r4w_sales_units, 0) AS SIGNED) AS r4w_sales_units,
    CAST(ROUND(sj.p4w_sales_units, 0) AS SIGNED) AS p4w_sales_units,
    nj.nation_r4w_sales_units,
    nj.nation_p4w_sales_units,
    CASE
      WHEN (COALESCE(sj.p4w_sales_units, 0) / NULLIF(pwc.p4w_week_count, 0)) = 0
           AND (COALESCE(sj.r4w_sales_units, 0) / NULLIF(rwc.r4w_week_count, 0)) > 0
        THEN 100.0
      WHEN (COALESCE(sj.p4w_sales_units, 0) / NULLIF(pwc.p4w_week_count, 0)) = 0
           AND (COALESCE(sj.r4w_sales_units, 0) / NULLIF(rwc.r4w_week_count, 0)) = 0
        THEN 0.0
      ELSE (
        (
          (COALESCE(sj.r4w_sales_units, 0) / NULLIF(rwc.r4w_week_count, 0)) -
          (COALESCE(sj.p4w_sales_units, 0) / NULLIF(pwc.p4w_week_count, 0))
        ) * 100.0
      ) / NULLIF((COALESCE(sj.p4w_sales_units, 0) / NULLIF(pwc.p4w_week_count, 0)), 0)
    END AS r4w_growth_num,
    CASE
      WHEN (nj.nation_p4w_sales_units / NULLIF(pwc.p4w_week_count, 0)) = 0
           AND (nj.nation_r4w_sales_units / NULLIF(rwc.r4w_week_count, 0)) > 0
        THEN 100.0
      WHEN (nj.nation_p4w_sales_units / NULLIF(pwc.p4w_week_count, 0)) = 0
           AND (nj.nation_r4w_sales_units / NULLIF(rwc.r4w_week_count, 0)) = 0
        THEN 0.0
      ELSE (
        (
          (nj.nation_r4w_sales_units / NULLIF(rwc.r4w_week_count, 0)) -
          (nj.nation_p4w_sales_units / NULLIF(pwc.p4w_week_count, 0))
        ) * 100.0
      ) / NULLIF((nj.nation_p4w_sales_units / NULLIF(pwc.p4w_week_count, 0)), 0)
    END AS nation_r4w_growth_num
  FROM segment_join_cte sj
  CROSS JOIN nation_join_cte nj
  CROSS JOIN r4w_week_count_cte rwc
  CROSS JOIN p4w_week_count_cte pwc
)
SELECT
  mapped_child_bc_segment,
  r4w_sales_units,
  p4w_sales_units,
  CONCAT(ROUND(r4w_growth_num, 0), '%') AS r4w_growth_pct,
  nation_r4w_sales_units,
  nation_p4w_sales_units,
  CONCAT(ROUND(nation_r4w_growth_num, 0), '%') AS nation_r4w_growth_pct,
  CONCAT(ROUND(r4w_growth_num - nation_r4w_growth_num, 0), '%') AS growth_vs_nation_delta_pct
FROM final_metrics_cte
ORDER BY mapped_child_bc_segment ASC;

2026-02-18T06:27:54.870899+00:00Z.question: what is the sales growth in recent 4 weeks by child account types and BC potential segments and how does that compare with nation?

query_decomposition.intent_summary: Compute sales growth for the most recent 4 weeks (R4W) by child account type and child BC potential segment, compare each group's growth to national (overall) growth, and include absolute R4W and P4W volumes for both group and nation.

subqueries.name: max_week_cte
subqueries.purpose: Identify the most recent week_end_date in the dataset for weekly window derivation.
subqueries.logic: Compute MAX(week_end_date) from drug_sales. Do not use system date.

subqueries.name: r4w_weeks_cte
subqueries.purpose: Derive the set of the most recent 4 distinct weeks for R4W based on the max week_end_date.
subqueries.logic: Select the 4 most recent distinct week_end_date values such that week_end_date <= (value from max_week_cte), ordered by week_end_date DESC, limited to 4. This forms the R4W week set.

subqueries.name: p4w_weeks_cte
subqueries.purpose: Derive the 4 distinct weeks immediately preceding the R4W window for P4W.
subqueries.logic: First compute min_r4w_week as MIN(week_end_date) from r4w_weeks_cte. Then select the 4 distinct week_end_date values where week_end_date < min_r4w_week, ordered by week_end_date DESC, limited to 4. This forms the P4W week set.

subqueries.name: base_enriched_cte
subqueries.purpose: Prepare transactional rows with required mappings and flags to support conditional aggregation for R4W and P4W.
subqueries.logic: Select from drug_sales with qty_sold_pu > 0. Derive child_account_type_bucket as: CASE WHEN child_account_type IN ('Unknown') THEN 'Community' WHEN child_account_type IN ('Other','Others') THEN 'IDN/Hospital' ELSE child_account_type END. Derive child_bc_segment_bucket as: CASE WHEN child_bc_segment IN ('Unknown','Others') THEN 'Low' ELSE child_bc_segment END. Add flags: in_r4w = 1 if week_end_date IN (set from r4w_weeks_cte) else 0; in_p4w = 1 if week_end_date IN (set from p4w_weeks_cte) else 0.

subqueries.name: group_metrics_cte
subqueries.purpose: Aggregate R4W and P4W sales volumes at the requested group level.
subqueries.logic: Group by child_account_type_bucket, child_bc_segment_bucket from base_enriched_cte. Compute r4w_sales = SUM(CASE WHEN in_r4w = 1 THEN qty_sold_pu ELSE 0 END) and p4w_sales = SUM(CASE WHEN in_p4w = 1 THEN qty_sold_pu ELSE 0 END).

subqueries.name: national_metrics_cte
subqueries.purpose: Compute national R4W and P4W volumes and national growth for benchmark comparison.
subqueries.logic: From base_enriched_cte without grouping, compute nation_r4w_sales = SUM(CASE WHEN in_r4w = 1 THEN qty_sold_pu ELSE 0 END) and nation_p4w_sales = SUM(CASE WHEN in_p4w = 1 THEN qty_sold_pu ELSE 0 END). Then compute nation_growth_pct (integer) with zero-denominator handling: IF nation_p4w_sales = 0 AND nation_r4w_sales > 0 THEN 100; ELSE IF nation_p4w_sales = 0 AND nation_r4w_sales = 0 THEN 0; ELSE ROUND(((nation_r4w_sales - nation_p4w_sales) * 100.0) / nation_p4w_sales).

subqueries.name: final_calc_cte
subqueries.purpose: Join group and national metrics and compute group growth and comparison to nation.
subqueries.logic: Select from group_metrics_cte and CROSS JOIN national_metrics_cte. Compute growth_pct (integer) per group with zero-denominator handling: IF p4w_sales = 0 AND r4w_sales > 0 THEN 100; ELSE IF p4w_sales = 0 AND r4w_sales = 0 THEN 0; ELSE ROUND(((r4w_sales - p4w_sales) * 100.0) / p4w_sales). Also carry nation_r4w_sales, nation_p4w_sales, nation_growth_pct from national_metrics_cte. Optionally compute growth_vs_nation_pp = growth_pct - nation_growth_pct (integer).

validation_rules: All weekly windows must be derived relative to MAX(week_end_date) from the data; do not use system date.
R4W must consist of the 4 most recent distinct week_end_date values.
P4W must consist of the 4 distinct week_end_date values immediately preceding the R4W window.
If R4W and P4W contain different counts of weeks due to data sparsity, normalize volumes to weekly averages before computing growth.
Restrict all computations to rows where qty_sold_pu > 0.
Weekly calculations must use week_end_date (not shipt_dt).
For child_account_type: merge 'Unknown' into 'Community'; if 'Other'/'Others' occur, merge into 'IDN/Hospital'.
For child_bc_segment: merge 'Unknown' and 'Others' into 'Low' before aggregation.
Use only child-level columns for this request; do not substitute or aggregate with parent-level attributes.
Growth formula: if previous period (P4W) = 0 and current (R4W) > 0, set growth to 100%; if both are 0, set growth to 0%; otherwise growth = ((R4W - P4W)/P4W)*100.
All growth and comparison percentage outputs must be rounded to zero decimals (integers) and have a trailing '%'.
Always accompany each growth percentage with its absolute R4W and P4W volume values.
Include national benchmark metrics (nation R4W, nation P4W, nation growth%) alongside each group for comparison.
All child entities must remain at child granularity; do not roll up to parent entities for this analysis.

2026-02-18T06:27:54.870899+00:00Z.final_sql:
WITH
max_week_cte AS (
  SELECT MAX(week_end_date) AS max_week
  FROM drug_sales
),
r4w_weeks_cte AS (
  SELECT week_end_date
  FROM (
    SELECT DISTINCT week_end_date
    FROM drug_sales
    WHERE week_end_date <= (SELECT max_week FROM max_week_cte)
    ORDER BY week_end_date DESC
    LIMIT 4
  ) x
),
p4w_weeks_cte AS (
  SELECT week_end_date
  FROM (
    SELECT DISTINCT week_end_date
    FROM drug_sales
    WHERE week_end_date < (SELECT MIN(week_end_date) FROM r4w_weeks_cte)
    ORDER BY week_end_date DESC
    LIMIT 4
  ) y
),
week_counts_cte AS (
  SELECT
    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM r4w_weeks_cte) AS r4w_weeks_count,
    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM p4w_weeks_cte) AS p4w_weeks_count
),
base_enriched_cte AS (
  SELECT
    CASE
      WHEN child_account_type IN ('Unknown') THEN 'Community'
      WHEN child_account_type IN ('Other','Others') THEN 'IDN/Hospital'
      ELSE child_account_type
    END AS child_account_type_bucket,
    CASE
      WHEN child_bc_segment IN ('Unknown','Others') THEN 'Low'
      ELSE child_bc_segment
    END AS child_bc_segment_bucket,
    qty_sold_pu,
    week_end_date,
    CASE WHEN week_end_date IN (SELECT week_end_date FROM r4w_weeks_cte) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS in_r4w,
    CASE WHEN week_end_date IN (SELECT week_end_date FROM p4w_weeks_cte) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS in_p4w
  FROM drug_sales
  WHERE qty_sold_pu > 0
),
group_metrics_cte AS (
  SELECT
    child_account_type_bucket,
    child_bc_segment_bucket,
    SUM(CASE WHEN in_r4w = 1 THEN qty_sold_pu ELSE 0 END) AS r4w_sales,
    SUM(CASE WHEN in_p4w = 1 THEN qty_sold_pu ELSE 0 END) AS p4w_sales
  FROM base_enriched_cte
  GROUP BY child_account_type_bucket, child_bc_segment_bucket
),
national_metrics_cte AS (
  SELECT
    SUM(CASE WHEN in_r4w = 1 THEN qty_sold_pu ELSE 0 END) AS nation_r4w_sales,
    SUM(CASE WHEN in_p4w = 1 THEN qty_sold_pu ELSE 0 END) AS nation_p4w_sales
  FROM base_enriched_cte
)
SELECT
  gm.child_account_type_bucket,
  gm.child_bc_segment_bucket,
  ROUND(gm.r4w_sales) AS r4w_sales,
  ROUND(gm.p4w_sales) AS p4w_sales,
  CONCAT(
    CAST(
      CASE
        WHEN (gm.p4w_sales = 0 OR wc.p4w_weeks_count = 0) AND (gm.r4w_sales > 0 AND wc.r4w_weeks_count > 0) THEN 100
        WHEN (gm.p4w_sales = 0 OR wc.p4w_weeks_count = 0) AND NOT (gm.r4w_sales > 0 AND wc.r4w_weeks_count > 0) THEN 0
        ELSE ROUND(
          (
            (gm.r4w_sales / NULLIF(wc.r4w_weeks_count, 0)) -
            (gm.p4w_sales / NULLIF(wc.p4w_weeks_count, 0))
          ) * 100.0 / NULLIF((gm.p4w_sales / NULLIF(wc.p4w_weeks_count, 0)), 0)
        )
      END AS SIGNED
    ),
    '%'
  ) AS growth_pct,
  ROUND(nm.nation_r4w_sales) AS nation_r4w_sales,
  ROUND(nm.nation_p4w_sales) AS nation_p4w_sales,
  CONCAT(
    CAST(
      CASE
        WHEN (nm.nation_p4w_sales = 0 OR wc.p4w_weeks_count = 0) AND (nm.nation_r4w_sales > 0 AND wc.r4w_weeks_count > 0) THEN 100
        WHEN (nm.nation_p4w_sales = 0 OR wc.p4w_weeks_count = 0) AND NOT (nm.nation_r4w_sales > 0 AND wc.r4w_weeks_count > 0) THEN 0
        ELSE ROUND(
          (
            (nm.nation_r4w_sales / NULLIF(wc.r4w_weeks_count, 0)) -
            (nm.nation_p4w_sales / NULLIF(wc.p4w_weeks_count, 0))
          ) * 100.0 / NULLIF((nm.nation_p4w_sales / NULLIF(wc.p4w_weeks_count, 0)), 0)
        )
      END AS SIGNED
    ),
    '%'
  ) AS nation_growth_pct,
  CONCAT(
    CAST(
      (
        CASE
          WHEN (gm.p4w_sales = 0 OR wc.p4w_weeks_count = 0) AND (gm.r4w_sales > 0 AND wc.r4w_weeks_count > 0) THEN 100
          WHEN (gm.p4w_sales = 0 OR wc.p4w_weeks_count = 0) AND NOT (gm.r4w_sales > 0 AND wc.r4w_weeks_count > 0) THEN 0
          ELSE ROUND(
            (
              (gm.r4w_sales / NULLIF(wc.r4w_weeks_count, 0)) -
              (gm.p4w_sales / NULLIF(wc.p4w_weeks_count, 0))
            ) * 100.0 / NULLIF((gm.p4w_sales / NULLIF(wc.p4w_weeks_count, 0)), 0)
          )
        END
        -
        (
          CASE
            WHEN (nm.nation_p4w_sales = 0 OR wc.p4w_weeks_count = 0) AND (nm.nation_r4w_sales > 0 AND wc.r4w_weeks_count > 0) THEN 100
            WHEN (nm.nation_p4w_sales = 0 OR wc.p4w_weeks_count = 0) AND NOT (nm.nation_r4w_sales > 0 AND wc.r4w_weeks_count > 0) THEN 0
            ELSE ROUND(
              (
                (nm.nation_r4w_sales / NULLIF(wc.r4w_weeks_count, 0)) -
                (nm.nation_p4w_sales / NULLIF(wc.p4w_weeks_count, 0))
              ) * 100.0 / NULLIF((nm.nation_p4w_sales / NULLIF(wc.p4w_weeks_count, 0)), 0)
            )
          END
        )
      ) AS SIGNED
    ),
    '%'
  ) AS growth_vs_nation_pp
FROM group_metrics_cte gm
CROSS JOIN week_counts_cte wc
CROSS JOIN national_metrics_cte nm
ORDER BY
  gm.child_account_type_bucket ASC,
  gm.child_bc_segment_bucket ASC;

2026-02-20T05:55:18.256388+00:00Z.question: Are there parent accounts with high sales contribution in recent 12 months but declining trends in recent 13 weeks?

query_decomposition.intent_summary: Identify parent accounts that have high national sales contribution over the most recent rolling 52 weeks (R12M) and show a declining sales trend in the most recent 13 weeks (R13W) versus the prior 13 weeks (P13W), anchored to the maximum week_end_date in the data.

subqueries.name: max_week_end_date_cte
subqueries.purpose: Identify the most recent week_end_date in the dataset to anchor all rolling windows.
subqueries.logic: Compute MAX(week_end_date) as max_week_end_date from drug_sales after applying the default filter qty_sold_pu > 0.

subqueries.name: windowed_parent_sales_cte
subqueries.purpose: Compute parent-level sales for R52W, R13W, and P13W using week_end_date windows anchored to max_week_end_date.
subqueries.logic: For each parent_eid and parent_name, sum qty_sold_pu separately for: (a) sales_r52w where week_end_date is within the last 52 weeks ending at max_week_end_date; (b) sales_r13w where week_end_date is within the last 13 weeks ending at max_week_end_date; (c) sales_p13w where week_end_date is within the 13 weeks immediately preceding the R13W window. Ensure qty_sold_pu > 0 is applied.

subqueries.name: national_sales_cte
subqueries.purpose: Compute national totals for the same windows to serve as denominators for contribution calculations and to include nation metrics in output.
subqueries.logic: Aggregate (no parent grouping) sum qty_sold_pu separately for national_sales_r52w, national_sales_r13w, and national_sales_p13w using the same R52W/R13W/P13W week_end_date window definitions anchored to max_week_end_date. Ensure qty_sold_pu > 0 is applied.

subqueries.name: scored_parent_accounts_cte
subqueries.purpose: Calculate contribution and trend metrics at the parent level and prepare numeric fields needed for filtering and ordering.
subqueries.logic: Join windowed_parent_sales_cte to national_sales_cte (cross join). Compute numeric metrics: contrib_r12m_pct_num = (sales_r52w / national_sales_r52w) * 100; weekly_avg_r13w_num = sales_r13w / 13; weekly_avg_p13w_num = sales_p13w / 13; trend_growth_r13w_vs_p13w_pct_num = CASE WHEN weekly_avg_p13w_num = 0 AND weekly_avg_r13w_num > 0 THEN 100 WHEN weekly_avg_p13w_num = 0 AND weekly_avg_r13w_num = 0 THEN 0 ELSE ((weekly_avg_r13w_num - weekly_avg_p13w_num) / weekly_avg_p13w_num) * 100 END. Also compute formatted outputs later as integer-rounded with '%' appended.

subqueries.name: high_contribution_threshold_cte
subqueries.purpose: Derive a single scalar threshold that defines 'high sales contribution' deterministically.
subqueries.logic: From scored_parent_accounts_cte, compute the 80th percentile of contrib_r12m_pct_num across all parent accounts and expose it as a single scalar column named contrib_r12m_pct_p80_num (one row).

subqueries.name: final_filtered_cte
subqueries.purpose: Filter to parent accounts that are both high contributors and declining in recent 13 weeks vs prior 13 weeks, without invalid aggregate usage.
subqueries.logic: Join scored_parent_accounts_cte to high_contribution_threshold_cte (cross join). Filter using scalar comparison only: scored_parent_accounts_cte.contrib_r12m_pct_num >= high_contribution_threshold_cte.contrib_r12m_pct_p80_num AND scored_parent_accounts_cte.trend_growth_r13w_vs_p13w_pct_num < 0. Do not apply any aggregate functions in the filter predicate.

validation_rules: Default aggregation level must be parent entity (parent_eid, parent_name).
All computations must be restricted to rows where qty_sold_pu > 0.
All rolling windows must be anchored to MAX(week_end_date) derived from the data; do not use system date.
R12M must be implemented as a rolling 52-week period and labeled as 12 months in output metric names (e.g., sales_r52w, contrib_r12m_pct).
R13W and P13W must be 13-week windows based on week_end_date; P13W is the 13 weeks immediately preceding R13W.
Since compared periods for trend are equal length (13 vs 13 weeks), normalization to weekly average is allowed; growth must be computed from weekly averages.
Contribution denominator must be overall national sales for the same window (national_sales_r52w).
All percentage metrics (contrib_r12m_pct and trend_growth_r13w_vs_p13w_pct) must be rounded to 0 decimals, returned as integers, and appended with '%'.
Whenever a growth metric is output, also output the corresponding absolute sales values for both periods (sales_r13w and sales_p13w).
If previous-period weekly average used in growth calculation is 0 and current-period weekly average is > 0, growth must be reported as 100%; if both are 0, report 0%.
Merge parent_account_type 'Other' into 'IDN/Hospital' and 'Unknown' into 'Community' if parent_account_type is used downstream; otherwise no account-type filtering is applied.
Return only parent accounts that meet both conditions: high contribution (contrib_r12m_pct_num >= scalar 80th percentile threshold) and declining trend (trend_growth_r13w_vs_p13w_pct_num < 0).
Filtering on the high contribution threshold must not use any aggregate functions in the final filter predicate; the percentile CTE must return a single scalar and be compared directly.

2026-02-20T05:55:18.256388+00:00Z.final_sql:
WITH
max_week_end_date_cte AS (
  SELECT
    MAX(week_end_date) AS max_week_end_date
  FROM drug_sales
  WHERE qty_sold_pu > 0
),
windowed_parent_sales_cte AS (
  SELECT
    ts.parent_eid,
    ts.parent_name,
    SUM(
      CASE
        WHEN ts.week_end_date BETWEEN DATE_SUB(m.max_week_end_date, INTERVAL 51 WEEK) AND m.max_week_end_date
        THEN ts.qty_sold_pu ELSE 0
      END
    ) AS sales_r52w,
    SUM(
      CASE
        WHEN ts.week_end_date BETWEEN DATE_SUB(m.max_week_end_date, INTERVAL 12 WEEK) AND m.max_week_end_date
        THEN ts.qty_sold_pu ELSE 0
      END
    ) AS sales_r13w,
    SUM(
      CASE
        WHEN ts.week_end_date BETWEEN DATE_SUB(m.max_week_end_date, INTERVAL 25 WEEK) AND DATE_SUB(m.max_week_end_date, INTERVAL 13 WEEK)
        THEN ts.qty_sold_pu ELSE 0
      END
    ) AS sales_p13w
  FROM drug_sales ts
  JOIN max_week_end_date_cte m
  WHERE ts.qty_sold_pu > 0
    AND ts.week_end_date >= DATE_SUB(m.max_week_end_date, INTERVAL 51 WEEK)
  GROUP BY
    ts.parent_eid,
    ts.parent_name
),
national_sales_cte AS (
  SELECT
    SUM(
      CASE
        WHEN ts.week_end_date BETWEEN DATE_SUB(m.max_week_end_date, INTERVAL 51 WEEK) AND m.max_week_end_date
        THEN ts.qty_sold_pu ELSE 0
      END
    ) AS national_sales_r52w,
    SUM(
      CASE
        WHEN ts.week_end_date BETWEEN DATE_SUB(m.max_week_end_date, INTERVAL 12 WEEK) AND m.max_week_end_date
        THEN ts.qty_sold_pu ELSE 0
      END
    ) AS national_sales_r13w,
    SUM(
      CASE
        WHEN ts.week_end_date BETWEEN DATE_SUB(m.max_week_end_date, INTERVAL 25 WEEK) AND DATE_SUB(m.max_week_end_date, INTERVAL 13 WEEK)
        THEN ts.qty_sold_pu ELSE 0
      END
    ) AS national_sales_p13w
  FROM drug_sales ts
  JOIN max_week_end_date_cte m
  WHERE ts.qty_sold_pu > 0
    AND ts.week_end_date >= DATE_SUB(m.max_week_end_date, INTERVAL 51 WEEK)
),
scored_parent_accounts_cte AS (
  SELECT
    w.parent_eid,
    w.parent_name,
    w.sales_r52w,
    w.sales_r13w,
    w.sales_p13w,
    n.national_sales_r52w,
    n.national_sales_r13w,
    n.national_sales_p13w,
    (w.sales_r52w / NULLIF(n.national_sales_r52w, 0)) * 100 AS contrib_r12m_pct_num,
    (w.sales_r13w / 13) AS weekly_avg_r13w_num,
    (w.sales_p13w / 13) AS weekly_avg_p13w_num,
    CASE
      WHEN (w.sales_p13w / 13) = 0 AND (w.sales_r13w / 13) > 0 THEN 100
      WHEN (w.sales_p13w / 13) = 0 AND (w.sales_r13w / 13) = 0 THEN 0
      ELSE (((w.sales_r13w / 13) - (w.sales_p13w / 13)) / NULLIF((w.sales_p13w / 13), 0)) * 100
    END AS trend_growth_r13w_vs_p13w_pct_num
  FROM windowed_parent_sales_cte w
  CROSS JOIN national_sales_cte n
),
high_contribution_threshold_cte AS (
  SELECT
    CAST(
      SUBSTRING_INDEX(
        SUBSTRING_INDEX(
          GROUP_CONCAT(contrib_r12m_pct_num ORDER BY contrib_r12m_pct_num SEPARATOR ','),
          ',',
          CEIL(0.8 * COUNT(*))
        ),
        ',',
        -1
      ) AS DECIMAL(18,6)
    ) AS contrib_r12m_pct_p80_num
  FROM scored_parent_accounts_cte
),
final_filtered_cte AS (
  SELECT
    s.*
  FROM scored_parent_accounts_cte s
  CROSS JOIN high_contribution_threshold_cte t
  WHERE s.contrib_r12m_pct_num >= t.contrib_r12m_pct_p80_num
    AND s.trend_growth_r13w_vs_p13w_pct_num < 0
)
SELECT
  parent_eid,
  parent_name,
  CAST(ROUND(sales_r52w, 0) AS SIGNED) AS sales_r52w,
  CONCAT(CAST(ROUND(contrib_r12m_pct_num, 0) AS SIGNED), '%') AS contrib_r12m_pct,
  CAST(ROUND(sales_r13w, 0) AS SIGNED) AS sales_r13w,
  CAST(ROUND(sales_p13w, 0) AS SIGNED) AS sales_p13w,
  CONCAT(CAST(ROUND(trend_growth_r13w_vs_p13w_pct_num, 0) AS SIGNED), '%') AS trend_growth_r13w_vs_p13w_pct,
  CAST(ROUND(national_sales_r52w, 0) AS SIGNED) AS national_sales_r52w,
  CAST(ROUND(national_sales_r13w, 0) AS SIGNED) AS national_sales_r13w,
  CAST(ROUND(national_sales_p13w, 0) AS SIGNED) AS national_sales_p13w
FROM final_filtered_cte
ORDER BY sales_r52w DESC
LIMIT 50;

2026-02-20T06:08:22.899081+00:00Z.question: Which accounts show sudden changes in sales behavior?

query_decomposition.intent_summary: Identify parent accounts with sudden changes in sales behavior by comparing Recent 4 Weeks (R4W) vs Prior 4 Weeks (P4W) weekly-average sales and reporting those with the largest absolute percentage change.

subqueries.name: max_week_end_date_cte
subqueries.purpose: Identify the most recent week_end_date in the dataset to anchor rolling windows.
subqueries.logic: Compute MAX(week_end_date) from drug_sales with qty_sold_pu > 0.

subqueries.name: window_tagging_cte
subqueries.purpose: Tag each transaction week_end_date into R4W or P4W relative to the max week_end_date.
subqueries.logic: Using max_week_end_date_cte.max_week_end_date, classify records where week_end_date is within the last 4 weeks (inclusive) as 'R4W' and the preceding 4 weeks as 'P4W'; exclude all other dates.

subqueries.name: account_period_sales_cte
subqueries.purpose: Aggregate sales by parent account and period (R4W vs P4W).
subqueries.logic: Group by parent_eid and parent_name and period_tag (R4W/P4W) and sum qty_sold_pu.

subqueries.name: account_change_metrics_cte
subqueries.purpose: Compute sudden-change metrics per parent account (weekly average normalization, growth %, and absolute growth %).
subqueries.logic: Pivot period sums into sales_r4w and sales_p4w per parent account; compute weekly_avg_r4w = sales_r4w/4 and weekly_avg_p4w = sales_p4w/4; compute growth_r4w_vs_p4w_pct as: if weekly_avg_p4w = 0 and weekly_avg_r4w > 0 then 100, else if weekly_avg_p4w = 0 and weekly_avg_r4w = 0 then 0, else ((weekly_avg_r4w - weekly_avg_p4w)/weekly_avg_p4w)*100; compute abs_growth_r4w_vs_p4w_pct = ABS(growth_r4w_vs_p4w_pct). Round all reported metrics to 0 decimals and keep as integers; append '%' to percentage outputs.

validation_rules: Default to parent-level aggregation because the user did not specify parent vs child.
Must restrict all computations to rows where qty_sold_pu > 0.
R4W and P4W must be defined relative to MAX(week_end_date) in the dataset; do not use system date.
Because comparison windows are equal length (4 vs 4 weeks), weekly average normalization must still be computed deterministically as sales/4 for both periods before growth calculation.
Growth must be expressed as a percentage with a '%' suffix; if previous period (weekly_avg_p4w) is 0 and current period (weekly_avg_r4w) > 0, growth must be 100%.
Always accompany the growth metric with corresponding absolute sales volumes for both periods in the output.
All metric outputs must be rounded to zero decimal places and returned as integers.
Use week_end_date for rolling weekly calculations.

2026-02-20T06:08:22.899081+00:00Z.final_sql:
WITH
max_week_end_date_cte AS (
  SELECT
    MAX(week_end_date) AS max_week_end_date
  FROM drug_sales
  WHERE qty_sold_pu > 0
),
window_tagging_cte AS (
  SELECT
    ts.parent_eid,
    ts.parent_name,
    ts.qty_sold_pu,
    CASE
      WHEN ts.week_end_date BETWEEN DATE_SUB(m.max_week_end_date, INTERVAL 3 WEEK) AND m.max_week_end_date THEN 'R4W'
      WHEN ts.week_end_date BETWEEN DATE_SUB(m.max_week_end_date, INTERVAL 7 WEEK) AND DATE_SUB(m.max_week_end_date, INTERVAL 4 WEEK) THEN 'P4W'
      ELSE NULL
    END AS period_tag
  FROM drug_sales ts
  CROSS JOIN max_week_end_date_cte m
  WHERE ts.qty_sold_pu > 0
    AND ts.week_end_date >= DATE_SUB(m.max_week_end_date, INTERVAL 7 WEEK)
),
account_period_sales_cte AS (
  SELECT
    parent_eid,
    parent_name,
    period_tag,
    SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS period_sales
  FROM window_tagging_cte
  WHERE period_tag IN ('R4W', 'P4W')
  GROUP BY parent_eid, parent_name, period_tag
),
account_change_metrics_cte AS (
  SELECT
    parent_eid,
    parent_name,
    COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN period_tag = 'R4W' THEN period_sales END), 0) AS sales_r4w,
    COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN period_tag = 'P4W' THEN period_sales END), 0) AS sales_p4w,
    (COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN period_tag = 'R4W' THEN period_sales END), 0) / 4) AS weekly_avg_sales_r4w,
    (COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN period_tag = 'P4W' THEN period_sales END), 0) / 4) AS weekly_avg_sales_p4w,
    CONCAT(
      ROUND(
        CASE
          WHEN (COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN period_tag = 'P4W' THEN period_sales END), 0) / 4) = 0
               AND (COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN period_tag = 'R4W' THEN period_sales END), 0) / 4) > 0
            THEN 100
          WHEN (COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN period_tag = 'P4W' THEN period_sales END), 0) / 4) = 0
               AND (COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN period_tag = 'R4W' THEN period_sales END), 0) / 4) = 0
            THEN 0
          ELSE
            (
              (
                (COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN period_tag = 'R4W' THEN period_sales END), 0) / 4)
                -
                (COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN period_tag = 'P4W' THEN period_sales END), 0) / 4)
              )
              /
              NULLIF((COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN period_tag = 'P4W' THEN period_sales END), 0) / 4), 0)
            ) * 100
        END
      ),
      '%'
    ) AS growth_r4w_vs_p4w,
    CONCAT(
      ROUND(
        ABS(
          CASE
            WHEN (COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN period_tag = 'P4W' THEN period_sales END), 0) / 4) = 0
                 AND (COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN period_tag = 'R4W' THEN period_sales END), 0) / 4) > 0
              THEN 100
            WHEN (COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN period_tag = 'P4W' THEN period_sales END), 0) / 4) = 0
                 AND (COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN period_tag = 'R4W' THEN period_sales END), 0) / 4) = 0
              THEN 0
            ELSE
              (
                (
                  (COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN period_tag = 'R4W' THEN period_sales END), 0) / 4)
                  -
                  (COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN period_tag = 'P4W' THEN period_sales END), 0) / 4)
                )
                /
                NULLIF((COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN period_tag = 'P4W' THEN period_sales END), 0) / 4), 0)
              ) * 100
          END
        )
      ),
      '%'
    ) AS abs_growth_r4w_vs_p4w,
    ROUND(
      ABS(
        CASE
          WHEN (COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN period_tag = 'P4W' THEN period_sales END), 0) / 4) = 0
               AND (COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN period_tag = 'R4W' THEN period_sales END), 0) / 4) > 0
            THEN 100
          WHEN (COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN period_tag = 'P4W' THEN period_sales END), 0) / 4) = 0
               AND (COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN period_tag = 'R4W' THEN period_sales END), 0) / 4) = 0
            THEN 0
          ELSE
            (
              (
                (COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN period_tag = 'R4W' THEN period_sales END), 0) / 4)
                -
                (COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN period_tag = 'P4W' THEN period_sales END), 0) / 4)
              )
              /
              NULLIF((COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN period_tag = 'P4W' THEN period_sales END), 0) / 4), 0)
            ) * 100
        END
      )
    ) AS abs_growth_r4w_vs_p4w_pct
  FROM account_period_sales_cte
  GROUP BY parent_eid, parent_name
)
SELECT
  parent_eid,
  parent_name,
  sales_r4w,
  sales_p4w,
  weekly_avg_sales_r4w,
  weekly_avg_sales_p4w,
  growth_r4w_vs_p4w,
  abs_growth_r4w_vs_p4w
FROM account_change_metrics_cte
ORDER BY abs_growth_r4w_vs_p4w_pct DESC, sales_r4w DESC
LIMIT 50;

2026-02-20T06:17:47.240192+00:00Z.question: Is there evidence of seasonality in sales behavior?

query_decomposition.intent_summary: Assess evidence of seasonality in national sales by comparing weekly average sales across month-of-year patterns over the most recent 52 weeks of data (parent level by default).

subqueries.name: max_shipt_dt_cte
subqueries.purpose: Identify the most recent shipment date available to anchor the default 52-week window.
subqueries.logic: Compute MAX(shipt_dt) from drug_sales with qty_sold_pu > 0.

subqueries.name: windowed_base_cte
subqueries.purpose: Restrict to the most recent 52 weeks of transactions for seasonality evaluation.
subqueries.logic: Filter drug_sales to qty_sold_pu > 0 and shipt_dt between (max_shipt_dt - 364 days) and max_shipt_dt.

subqueries.name: weekly_sales_cte
subqueries.purpose: Create weekly totals to support weekly-average normalization and month rollups.
subqueries.logic: From windowed_base_cte, aggregate SUM(qty_sold_pu) by week_end_date and month_year.

subqueries.name: monthly_seasonality_cte
subqueries.purpose: Compute month-level weekly average sales to normalize unequal month lengths and enable seasonal pattern comparison.
subqueries.logic: From weekly_sales_cte, for each month_year compute: (SUM(weekly_sales) / COUNT(DISTINCT week_end_date)) as month_weekly_avg_sales_52w; also compute monthly total sales and distinct weeks in month.

validation_rules: Default time window is the most recent 52 weeks when not specified by the user.
Must derive max_shipt_dt using a subquery; do not use system date.
All computations must be restricted to rows where qty_sold_pu > 0.
Use shipt_dt for the 52-week window restriction; month grouping must use month_year aligned to shipt_dt.

If time periods compared are not equal length, normalize metrics to a weekly average before comparisons; enforce this by using month_weekly_avg_sales_52w = SUM(weekly_sales)/COUNT(DISTINCT week_end_date).
All metric outputs must be rounded to zero decimal places and returned as integers (month_weekly_avg_sales_52w, sales_52w, weeks_in_month_52w).
Do not introduce child-level rollups; default is parent level, but this query is national and should not group by parent or child identifiers.
Seasonality evidence should be inferred downstream by comparing month_weekly_avg_sales_52w across months; this spec outputs the normalized monthly series required for that assessment.

2026-02-20T06:17:47.240192+00:00Z.final_sql:
WITH max_shipt_dt_cte AS (
  SELECT MAX(shipt_dt) AS max_shipt_dt
  FROM drug_sales
  WHERE qty_sold_pu > 0
),
windowed_base_cte AS (
  SELECT
    ts.shipt_dt,
    ts.week_end_date,
    ts.month_year,
    ts.qty_sold_pu
  FROM drug_sales ts
  JOIN max_shipt_dt_cte m
    ON 1 = 1
  WHERE ts.qty_sold_pu > 0
    AND ts.shipt_dt >= DATE_SUB(m.max_shipt_dt, INTERVAL 364 DAY)
    AND ts.shipt_dt <= m.max_shipt_dt
),
weekly_sales_cte AS (
  SELECT
    month_year,
    week_end_date,
    SUM(qty_sold_pu) AS weekly_sales
  FROM windowed_base_cte
  GROUP BY
    month_year,
    week_end_date
),
monthly_seasonality_cte AS (
  SELECT
    month_year,
    SUM(weekly_sales) AS sales_52w,
    COUNT(DISTINCT week_end_date) AS weeks_in_month_52w,
    SUM(weekly_sales) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT week_end_date), 0) AS month_weekly_avg_sales_52w
  FROM weekly_sales_cte
  GROUP BY month_year
)
SELECT
  month_year,
  CAST(ROUND(sales_52w, 0) AS SIGNED) AS sales_52w,
  CAST(ROUND(weeks_in_month_52w, 0) AS SIGNED) AS weeks_in_month_52w,
  CAST(ROUND(month_weekly_avg_sales_52w, 0) AS SIGNED) AS month_weekly_avg_sales_52w
FROM monthly_seasonality_cte
ORDER BY month_year ASC;
